What are you doing? We need to attack them.
What did my mother say exactly? Did she have urgent business with me?
What would you have me do?
Why isn't it here now? They're coming.
You'll kiss it again when I return and taste my uncle's blood.
Your king rides forth to battle. You should see him off with a kiss.

For your good service and ingenuity in uniting the Houses of Lannister and Tyrell, I declare that you shall be granted the castle of Harrenhal with all its attendant lands and incomes to be held by your sons and grandsons from this day until the end of time.
I would like to heed your wishes and the wishes of my people, but I took a holy vow.

I, Joffrey of the House Baratheon, first of my name, the rightful King of the Andals and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm, do hereby proclaim my grandfather, Tywin Lannister, the savior of the city and the Hand of the King.
I, too, have heard tales of your beauty and grace, but the tales do not do you justice, my lady. It would be an honor to return your love, but I am promised to another. A king must keep his word.
Is this what you want, Lady Margaery?
Lord Petyr Baelish, step forward.
The gods are good. I am free to heed my heart. Ser Loras, I will gladly wed your sweet sister. You will be my queen and I will love you from this day until my last day.
Your house has come to our aid. The whole realm is in your debt, none more so than I. If your family would ask anything of me, ask it, and it shall be yours.
Ser Loras Tyrell.
Please, sit. Sit. I do apologize, my lady. Small council meetings. At what point does it become treason to waste the king's time?
Shall I have them bring you a shawl, my lady?
That's a lovely gown, my lady.
Well, as Ser Loras said, Lady Margaery has done this sort of... charitable work before. I'm sure she knows what she's doing.
Joffrey: What is she doing? Who gave her permission?
My mother's always had a penchant for drama. Facts become less and less important to her as she grows older. Our lives were never truly in danger.
No.
Why have we stopped?
No, he was a known degenerate.

She's an ideal match. With the Tyrells beside us, we'll crush the Northerners. Hang their lords, burn their strongholds, sow their fields with salt, and no one will think of rebelling for another century.
Yes, she is.
And the bedside of a traitor? Is that a place for a lady?
And then you just aim and
Better. Much better.
Bloody flowers. On the king.
Could you do it? Could you kill something?.
Do you like it? I just had it made. Probably one of the finest weapons in the Seven Kingdoms.
Good. Good. How are you finding life in the capital? It must be quite a change after Renly's camp.
I don't need to do anything.
I'm leaving on a hunting trip. I just wanted to make sure you had everything you need before I left.
It's a new design. Much easier to load. There's no crank. You use this lever to draw the string.
It's not unheard of.
Make it tighter here.
Mmm. I've considered making his perversion punishable by death.
No flowers. I said no flowers. All these are wrong. Bring others.
Not to me.
Send her in.
She married Renly Baratheon because she was told to. That's what intelligent women do-what they're told.
The bolt goes here.
This is becoming one of the most boring conversations I've ever had.
What makes you say this?
What was your duty to this traitor as you saw it?
Who?
Yes.
Yes.
Yes. I am.
You failed to do this.
You think one ought to speak kindly of a traitor merely because he's had a sword put through his heart?
He was wrong. And, of course, there's the Mad King, killed by my uncle. Would you like to see where the last Targaryens are buried?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: My father didn't want them here. He was going to have their bodies burned and thrown in the Blackwater, but the High Septon convinced him otherwise.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Over there in that urn, the ashes of Aerion Targaryen. Aerion Brightflame they called him. He thought drinking wildfire would turn him into a dragon.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Rhaenyra Targaryen was murdered by her brother, or rather his dragon. It ate her while her son watched. What's left of her is buried in the crypts right down there.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I couldn't agree more.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Open the doors.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: The tomb's just up here.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: So if I wanted to attend a council meeting, I would now have to climb all the stairs in the Tower of the Hand?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: And the biggest was the size of a carriage.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: But how do we know these dragons are just curiosities and not the beasts that brought the whole world to heel?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: But I haven't been counseled.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Don't you think we ought to do something about it?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Is it true?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: May I ask why?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Tell me about the Targaryen girl in the east and her dragons.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Yes. I'd like a report on the meetings of my small council.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You've been holding the council meetings in the Tower of the Hand instead of the small council chamber.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Your father's gone. As the father of the realm, it is my duty to give you away to your husband.
Congratulations, my lady.
Well, you've done it. You've married a Lannister. Soon you'll have a Lannister baby. It's a dream come true for you, isn't it?  What a glorious day.

Where's your respect for tradition, Uncle? Come, everyone. Pick her up and carry her to her wedding bed. Get rid of her gown. She won't be needing it any longer. Ladies, attend to my uncle. He's not heavy.
I suppose it doesn't really matter which Lannister puts the baby into you. Maybe I'll pay you a visit tonight after my uncle passes out. How'd you like that?
I've got a lifetime for that.
There will be if I command it.
Time for the bedding ceremony.
What did you say? What did you say?
You wouldn't? That's all right. Ser Meryn and Ser Boros will hold you down.
Everyone is mine to torment. You'd do well to remember that, you little monster.
Show him. Come on, show him.
Yes, I did. I'm going to have it served to Sansa at my wedding feast.
I am the king! I will punish you.
I could have your tongue out for saying that.
I'm not tired!
I'm not tired.
My father won the real war. He killed Prince Rhaegar. He took the crown while you hid under Casterly Rock!

Robb Stark is dead. And his bitch mother. (to Pycelle) Write back to Lord Frey. Thank him for his service and command him to send Robb Stark's head. I'm going to serve it to Sansa at my wedding feast.
I broke Stannis on the Blackwater. Pity you weren't there to help, Uncle.
Yes, yes, one guard at the... the thing. Go on.
Busy getting captured.
By my leave. They know I saved the city. They know I won the war.
Is there? For a 40-year-old knight with one hand? How can you protect me with that?
Ser Jaime Lannister. (Chuckles)
Someone forgot to write down all your great deeds.
A handsome goblet, my lord. Or shall I call you Father?

Everyone, silence! Clear the floor. There's been too much amusement here today. A royal wedding is not an amusement. A royal wedding is history. The time has come for all of us to contemplate our history. My lords... my ladies...
I give you. King Joffrey... Renly, Stannis, Robb Stark, Balon Greyjoy. The War of the Five Kings.
Mm, good. Needs washing down.
No, no, no. No, you're perfect the way you are. Serve me my wine.
No. (coughing) No, you'll wait here... un...
Now that the war is won, we should all find time for wisdom. Thank you, Uncle.
Uncle. Where are you going? You're my cupbearer, remember?

Well fought. Well fought. Here you are. Champion's purse. Though you're not the champion yet, are you? A true champion defeats all the challengers. Surely there are others out there who still dare to challenge my reign. Uncle How about you? I'm sure they have a spare costume.
Well, how does he expect me to toast without wine? Uncle, you can be my cupbearer seeing as you're too cowardly to fight.
Well, hurry up. This pie is dry.
Widow's Wail. I like that. Every time I use it, it'll be like cutting off. Ned Stark's head all over again.
With this kiss, I pledge my love.
Yes, yes.
(gags)
A book?
A gold dragon to whoever knocks my fool's hat off.
A shame. I'd knight the man that put an end to that deviant's life.
Bring me my goblet.
Everyone. The queen would like to say a few words.
It did not spill.
It's not meant as an honor.
It's nothing.
Kneel. I said... kneel!
Kneel. Kneel before your king.
My queen.
So they say.
Such a great sword should have a name. What shall I call her?
Very good. Very good. Off you go.
What good is an empty cup? Fill it.
You're the one who put a sword through Renly Baratheon.
Ready, again.
Charge!
Charge! Yes!
Fine gentlemen.
For the Seven Kingdoms!
Let the war begin.
Yes!
Your head!
Here’s a silver because the lady likes you.
No, the raven is something different, something deeper. The raven brings the sight.
(Nods.) When I told my father about your father for the first time in my life, I saw him cry.
I'm Jojen Reed. This is my sister Meera. We've come a long way to find you, Brandon. And we have much farther to go.
You can get inside his head, see through his eyes.
You can't kill it, you know.
Your father told you about the rebellion? Mine never did. But I saw that, too.
A three-eyed raven?
Because the raven is you.
I'm unarmed.
My sister carries the weapons.
Only one thing that matters-you.
Or things that happened long before you were born or things that are happening right now thousands of miles away.
That's how it begins until you learn to control it. You're a warg.
We saw him together. You haven't forgotten?
Yeah.
You didn't dream it. You saw it. So did I.
You must be Summer.
You have to go after him.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You know how.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

I saw Jon Snow.
I don't fill his head with anything.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: It's not like that. I wish I could tell him all the answers. It would be much easier.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: That's where we are.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Shhh, quiet, Hodor.
You just did, with him.
You’re a warg, Bran.  It’s in your blood.
[Looks at BRAN]  The wolves are out there.
[To BRAN] Make him shut up.  If they hear us…
A wolf is nothing compared to that.
Do it.  They’ll find us if you don’t.
Doesn’t seem to be supporting anyone at the moment.
Just one?
Nineteen castles guard the Wall.  Only three are still manned.
No one can do that.  Anywhere.
Of course you can.  North of the Wall there are wildlings who can control all sorts of animals.  But you did a lot more than that.  You got inside Hodor’s mind.
One of those abandoned fortresses will give us a way through.
There’s a storm coming.
They’re gonna hear us.
Wildlings.
Doesn't matter.
Dragonglass.
How did you get through the Wall?
I always quite liked the horrible stories.
Let's find a place to sleep.
Shush.
The Night's Watch can't stop them. The kings of Westeros and all their armies can't stop them.
There's nowhere safe any longer. You know that.
Where are you going?
You saw the white walkers and the army of the dead.
Must be glorious, though. To run, to leap, to hunt. To be whole. I know that's tempting, but if you're trapped in Summer for too long, you'll forget what it was to be human.
Summer was eating. Your body can't live on the food your wolf consumes. Spending too much time in Summer's skin is dangerous. You're not a direwolf, Bran.
Bran. Bran.
It's coming.
If he sees you, he won't let you go north.
If you let my sister go, I can help you.
Meera and I, even Hodor, we're only here to guide you. He's waiting for you. We have to find it. You have to make it.
And wants to protect you. He'll take you back to Castle Black.
At the hill. A great weirwood tree.
I can.
I have the sight. I can see things.
I saw you die tonight. I saw your body burn. I saw the snow fall and bury your bones.
It doesn't matter.
No.
No. You're not here. You're far from here.
Stop. What are you doing?
Things that haven't happened yet.
This... this isn't the end. Not for you. Not yet.
You have to decide. Do you want to find the three-eyed raven?
You mustn't let anything stop you.
You'll know.
Bran! Save yourself, now!
Go with them.
We'll rest with the three-eyed raven.
We're already here.
Don’t think too much, Bran.
Father will let me if you ask him, I know he will.
Quick, Bran, faster!
And your mother.
Don’t look away.
Father will know if you do.
Go on. Father’s watching.
I don’t care about that.
I’m not a Stark. Get on.
I’m ready to swear your oath.
Lady Stark thought it might insult the royal family to seat a bastard in their midst.
Lord Eddard Stark is my father.
Lord Stark? There are five pups. One for each of the Stark children. The direwolf is the sigil of your House. They were meant to have them.
Now there are five.
So take me with you when you go back.
Uncle Benjen!
What is it?
What the hell do you know about being a bastard?
What’re you doing back there?
Why’s your mother so dead-set on us getting pretty for the king?
You did well.
You want to hold it?
You’re Tyrion Lannister. The queen’s brother?
And you, Stark.

I wish I could be here when you wake up. I'm going north with Uncle Benjen. I'm taking the black. I know we always talked about seeing the Wall together, but you'll be able to come visit me at Castle Black when you're better. I'll know my way around by then. I'll be a sworn brother of the Night's Watch. We can go out walking beyond the Wall, if you're not afraid.
I'm going to miss you... Careful... All the best swords have names, you know.
Ask me nicely and maybe I'll tell you, dwarf.
Is my mother alive? Does she know about me? Where I am, where I'm going? Does she care?
So are you. I had the blacksmith make it for you special. It won't hack a man's head off, but it can poke him full of holes if you're quick enough.
Close the door. This is no toy. Be careful you don't cut yourself.
First lesson: Stick them with the pointy end.
I already have one.
I came to say goodbye to Bran.
I have something for you. And it has to be packed very carefully.
Impressive.
Is this a trick?
It was always my color.
It's good you've got help.
Of course I have.
She was very kind.
Soon enough.
The Night's Watch protects the realm from...
Until your brother killed that King.
We've guarded the Kingdoms for 8 000 years.
Why do you read so much?
You Starks are hard to kill.
You'll have to work at it every day. How does it feel? Do you like the balance?
I'm sorry to see you leave, Lannister.
If you see my brother Bran, tell him I miss him. Tell him I'd visit if I could.
Farewell, My Lord.
But I'm better than every...
Don't stand so still. It's harder to hit a moving target. Except for you. You move too much. I could just hold my sword out and let you do the work for me.
Everybody knew what this place was and no one told me. No one but you. My father knew and left me to rot here at the Wall all the same.
He'll never walk again.
It's an improvement.
m ready. I won't let you down.
They hate me because I'm better than they are.
What kind of reports?
Will you stop at Winterfell on your way South?
You're leaving?
As a matter of fact, I'm the same as you.
I came very close once. I was alone in a room with a naked girl, but...
Yes, he is.
You can't fight. You can't see. You're afraid of heights and almost everything else probably. What are you doing here, Sam?

I never met my mother. My father wouldn't even tell me her name. I don't know if she's living or dead. I don't know if she's a noblewoman or a fisherman's wife... Or a whore. So I sat there in the brothel as Ros took off her clothes. But I couldn't do it. Because all I could think was what if I got her pregnant and she had a child, another bastard named Snow? It's not a good life for a child.
It's not going to get any easier, you know? You'll have to defend yourself.
Leg, shoulder, leg. Left foot forward. Good. Now pivot as you deliver the stroke. Put all your weight behind it.

That's enough. Sam's no different from the rest of us. There was no place for him in the world, so he's come here. We're not going to hurt him in the training yard anymore. Never again, no matter what Thorne says. He's our brother now and we're going to protect him.
And why is my surname Snow?
Are you sure you want to do this?
Better.
Come stand by the fire. It's warmer.
Enough! He yielded.
He wasn't hungry.
I build my own fires.
I didn't think you'd be so upset about it.
I know where to put it.
I wouldn't doubt it.
I've had worse.
No one touches Sam.
Red.
Sally on the side?
The Wall's no place for cowards.
Watch duty. With Sam.
Well… You can't get any worse.
What's my name?
You don't want to know.
You're not. You're freezing.
Young and gorgeous. A whore named Ros.
I do, my lord.
I just, I always wanted to be a Ranger.
I see Ser Alliser's revenge, that's all.
Yes, my lord.
Do you take me for a servant?
Hear my words and bear witness to my vow.
I want to find my Uncle.
May I go?
That's my Uncle Benjen's horse.
There's no rider.
Why is he alone?
Will I serve the Lord Commander's meals and fetch hot water for his bath.
Commander!Othor gets back on his feet, pulling out Jon's sword out of his chest. Outside of the room, Ghost goes beserk, trying to get in.
Ghost, what's wrong? Is something out there?
Just these two, My Lord. Been dead awhile, I'd say.
Commander?
Hello?
How do you know that?
Is there any word of my father?
Move move!
My sisters were in King's Landing too.
Stay.
We should burn them.
What else did the book say?
He would do whatever was right, no matter what.
My lord, you honor me, but l can't –
Soon, he says.
Yes, my lord.
Yes, my lord.
You do not know. No one knows. l may be a bastard, but he is my father and Robb is my brother.
And this is my day? ls that what you're saying?
And yet you really want to say. You want to say that…
At what?
l should be there. l should be with him.
l'll apologize to Ser Alliser tonight.
No.
Sam said you wanted to see me?
Sam told you.
This is Valyrian steel.
What is it?
What? What about him?
Who are you?
You can't what?
You're Aemon Targaryen.
Get out of my way, Sam.
Better than you do.
Beyond the Wall?
Ghost?
Hyah!
I don't care about –
l belong with my brother.
l have to.
l'm gonna find my brother and put a sword through King Joffrey's throat. (gets on horseback)
Move.
Move.
My friends brought me back.
No.
No.
They killed my father.
They'll kill you if they know you came after me. Go back.
To hell with all of you.
(Standing off to the side of the room) We're not Southerners.
Having a rough time of it?
He marries his daughters. What does he do with his sons?
Jon Snow.
Jon Snow.
Lord commander.
What happens to the boys?
Your steward.
Do what, ask her a question?
Hello, Gilly. What’re you doing?
I'm sorry Sam, we can't help her.
I'm sorry, Sam knows we're not supposed to -
If it's a boy what? (Gilly looks around and then down, not answering). You want us to risk our lives for you, and you won't even tell us why.
No it doesn't sound a bit mad, it's impossible.
We're heading deeper and deeper into wildling territory. We can't take a girl with us. Mormont wouldn't have it. And even if he would, what would we do with her? Who's gonna deliver her baby? You?
Cruel! Sam are you in such a hurry to lose a hand?
It's just not possible.
No you just want to steal her. What do you think Craster cuts off for that?
Sam we can't take her -
What happens to the boys? What does he do with his sons?
What? (Stands up).
What’re you doing?
I followed him. He took the baby into the woods, the newborn.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: No, you don't understand. He's killing them, all the boys.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I saw it. I saw something take that child.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Lord Commander
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: You know.

Text: Lord Commander, I'd like to join Lord Qhorin.
A fire.
I heard the Halfhand spent half of last winter beyond the Wall.
I think they were afraid. I think they came here to get away from something. And I don't think it worked.
I've fought and killed a wight. How many rangers can say that?
Look. It's Qhorin Halfhand.
My uncle told me stories about him.
One blast is for rangers returning. Wildlings is two blasts.
So it is possible for someone to survive out here on their own.
When will he come?
Where?
Ghost, stay with us. Ghost!

Text: I'm as much a crow as they are.

Text: I'm Jon Snow.

Text: No fire.

Text: No fire.

Text: If you're my prisoner, you're not a free woman. That's what "prisoner" means.
Invade your lands? Wildlings raid our lands all the time. Some of them tried to kill my little brother, a crippled boy!
My father was Ned Stark. I have the blood of the First Men. My ancestors lived here, same as yours.
No. No, you serve Mance Rayder, the King beyond the Wall.
There are no women of the Night's Watch, no.
You're a free woman? I might be your prisoner, but I'm a free woman.
Don't call them that.
Enough!
I know how to do it.
I'm a man of the Night's Watch.
It was my choice to say the words.
Let's move.
Move!
Neither. Both.
Never. We swore an oath.
No.
Nothing happened between you and me.
Of course I like girls. But you chose never to touch them.
That's enough.
That's the price you pay if you want to be a man of the Night's Watch.
Turn back around.
Walk.
We're close.
We're close.
Would you please shut up?
I couldn't do it. I had the sword on her neck, I just couldn't
I didn't.
The others?
They died because of me.
They'll never trust me.
What?
Never swung a sword before, have you? You look like a baby with a rattle.
Careful with that. You might cut yourself.
Stop!
If I'm a traitor, then you are, too.

Because when I told the Lord Commander, he already knew. Thousands of years ago, the First Men battled the White Walkers and defeated them. I want to fight for the side that fights for the living. Did I come to the right place?
When I'm free, will I be free to go?
(Hesitates.) I want to be free.
(Nods)
He wants me to lead one day.
I saw Craster take his own baby boy and leave it in the woods. I saw what took it.
Jon Snow.
Mance was a ranger.
My father told me big men fall just as quick as little ones if you put a sword through their hearts.
The Lord Commander sent me to the Halfhand for seasoning.
We stopped at Craster's Keep on the way north. I saw...
What happened to their fathers?
Your Grace.
Don't think too much, Bran.
I understand.
No.
Relax your bow arm.
What's wrong with him?
Yes.
You want to protect your people.
(Nods)
About 300.
Do you think anyone got away?
It's only horses. No men.
It varies. If I knew where on the Wall we were heading, I could tell you.
Yeah, thanks. I think I've heard enough. We'd best get back. Tormund--
(nods)
1000 men
Castle Black.
Eastwatch-by-the-Sea and the Shadow Tower.
Give it back.
I don't know. I just wanted to kiss you there is all.
I don't need you to protect me.
I was a man of the Night's Watch. How about you? Were you a maid?
There's been no one else. Only you.
Three.
We shouldn't.
What happens to your eagle after I kill you? Does he drift away like a kite with his strings cut or does he just flop dead to the ground?
Who was he?
Ygritte! Seven hells. Ygritte!
Ygritte! Ygritte!
Ygritte.
You seemed to like it.
(grunts)
(grunts)
(screams)
Aren't you?
Can we not talk about that here?
Did I seem scared the other day?
I won't.
Only in the beginning.
Take my hand!
What secret?
You ever climbed it before?
You kill someone for them?
About a week, I think.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Every boy in the North knows it. We grow up learning it. Where the battles were fought, the names of the heroes, who died where. Six times you've invaded and six times you've failed. The seventh will be the same.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Most of the time, yes.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: They don't, really. It's a great honor to carry your house sigil.

Text: I do. I know it. If you attack the Wall, you'll die. All of you.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: If you're impressed by a windmill, you'd be swooning if you saw the Great Keep at Winterfell.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: No. No, you're just gonna light the biggest fire the North has ever seen to let them know you're coming.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Six times in the last thousand years, a King has attacked the kingdoms. Six times they failed.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Yes, first we'll live.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Ygritte. You won't win. I know your people are brave, no one denies that.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: And are you gonna share it with me? The deep wisdom you found inside the head of a bird?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Fainting.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I know you cut me loose on the Wall.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I'd like to see you in a silk dress.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: It's a windmill.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: It's too far away.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Just the men who used to live here.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Maybe one day I'll take you to Winterfell.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: So I could tear it off you.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: They help the men march.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Well not all girls are like you.

Text: When a girl sees blood and collapses.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Winterfell has towers three times that size.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You don't have the discipline. You don't have the training. Your army is no army. You don't know how to fight together.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You're gonna scare it off.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: He breeds them for the Watch.
The Watch might send a few men looking for a horse thief.  They’ll send a lot more to hunt down murderers.
The Watch protects him.
We just take the horses and go.  The old man’s no threat.
Ygritte!
You were right the whole time.
Ygritte, you know I didn't have a choice. You always knew who I was, what I am.
I do know some things. I know I love you.
I have to go home now. I know you won't hurt me.
I know you love me. But I have to go home now.
Pyp? Sam?
100,000. He's united the Thenns, the Hornfoots, the ice-river clans. He has giants fighting for him.
Aye, I talk like a wildling. I ate with the wildlings. I climbed the Wall with the wildlings. I... I laid with a wildling girl.
Have you ever been beyond the Wall, ser?

Last time I saw him, he was in the courtyard at Winterfell. "He said, " Next time I see you, you'll be all in black." I was jealous of Robb my whole life. (Winces) The way my father looked at him, I wanted that. He was better than me at everything... fighting and hunting and riding and girls. Gods, the girls loved him. I wanted to hate him, but I never could.
Then you'd know he'd do anything to defend the Wall. The free folk would have boiled him alive, but letting me kill him...
While we sit here debating which rules I broke, Mance Rayder marches on the Wall with an army of 100,000.
(Chuckles)
And now you're here. You must not have been very good at your job.
Do you intend to execute me or am I free to go?
He wanted me to kill him.
He's wanted to hang me for a while. Now's his chance.
I didn't murder him.
I do.
I've done plenty wrong.
The Halfhand believed our only chance to stop Mance was to get a man inside his army.

There's a band of wildlings south of the Wall already led by Tormund Giantsbane. I killed their warg and three others. They shot me full of arrows. Their orders are to attack Castle Black from the south when Mance hits it from the north. The signal for the attack will be a bonfire. Mance said it would be the greatest fire the North has ever seen. That's the truth. All the truth.

It's not about justice. I told the wildlings we had over 1,000 men at Castle Black alone. Karl and the others know the truth as well as we do. How long do you think they'll keep that information to themselves when the wildlings are peeling their fingernails off? Mance has all he needs to crush us, he just doesn't know it yet. As soon as he gets his hands on them, he will. Then he'll throw his full strength at us. And even if every one of us kills 100 wildlings, there's still not a thing we can do to stop them.
Mance Rayder is coming. If the wildlings breach the Wall, they'll roll over everything and everyone for 1,000 Miles before they reach an army that can stop them.
And the mutineers stayed?
Help him.
We need to ride north and kill them all.
Ahem, brothers.

And I remember who came after me and brought me home. I know how hard it is, Sam, believe me. When you told me about Bran going beyond the Wall, all I could think about was getting my strength back so I could go and find him.

I'm going beyond the Wallto Craster's Keep. I'm going to capture the mutineers holed up there or kill them. I'm asking for volunteers to come with me. There's 60 miles of wilderness between here and Craster's and Mance Rayder has an army bearing down on us, but we have to do this. Our survival may depend on us getting to these mutineers before Mance does. They know the Wall. They know our defenses. If Mance learns what they know, we're lost. But if that's not enough, then consider this. If the Night's Watch are truly brothers, then Lord Commander Mormont was our father. He lived and died for the Watch and he was betrayed by his own men. Stabbed in the back by cowards. He deserved far better. All we can give him now is justice. Who will join me?
Olly, you just watch for now.
Thank you, brothers.
Thank you, Ser Alliser.
You two. Take it slow,  try and disarm each other.
A lot of wildlings fight with a weapon in each hand. First thing you want to do is disarm them to try and even the odds.
Bring it through! And again.
Castle Black could be next.
Grenn and I were helping them.
Have you ever held a sword before? OLLY I was the best archer in our hamlet.
How fast could they travel? A crippled boy being pulled on a sledge by a simpleton?
I can't let a recruit come north of the Wall.
Jon Snow.
Let's see what you can do.
My father was highborn. My mother wasn't.
She wasn't safe here.
Someone has to train them.
The wildlings have joined up with Mance. Every village or sheltered place will be deserted. Except...
Very nice. Good.
We have orders. No one's to leave the castle.
You fight well.
You know how to fight. You could have gone easier on him.
I missed you, boy.
(SIGHS) Get some rest. We move at sundown.
Are you all right? Come with me. Come on.
How many?
I count 10 dead mutineers.
It's not safe for you here on your own. Mance Rayder has an army heading this way and there's worse out there than Mance. Come with us to Castle Black. We can find you work. Keep you safe.
We lost four brothers?
Where in seven hells? Come here.
Why?
You want to stay here in Craster's Keep?
Come on, Ghost.
Have you ever seen a giant, Ser Alliser? I have.
Greetings.
Mance's army was closing in on Craster's Keep when we left. We saw their campfires from Osric's Hill. They'll reach the Wall before the next full moon.
No.
The builders.
The stewards.
They won't stop them.
We need to prepare.
We should seal the tunnel. Plug it with rocks and ice. Flood it and let it freeze.
We wouldn't.
If they hit Mole's Town, then we're next. Mance's army must be close.
We can't go after them, you know that. That's what they want.
You couldn't have known.
*to Grenn* The outer gate won't hold. Take five men, hold the inner gate.
Aye, you should've.
But if she had, you would've? You'd have broken your vows?
Ed. You have The Wall. If they try the mammoths again, drop fire on them. If the climbers get too high, drop the scythe on them.
Go get some sleep, Sam. I'll take this watch.

Great victory? Mance was testing our defenses. He almost made it through. And he has more giants, he has more mammoths. He has a thousand times as many men. They'll hit us again tonight. Maybe we can hold them off for a day or two, but we can never beat them.
It was a difficult decision either way, Ser.

It's...there's this person, this whole other person. And you're wrapped up in them and they're wrapped up in you and...you...for a little...for a little while you're more than just you, you're...well I don't know, I'm not a bleedin' poet!
No, shh, don't talk.
There we go, boys. That's one. There's two. Come on, let's get the next lot. That's the last of the oil, Ser Alliser.
They held the gate. Get some brothers down here to help you. You need to burn the bodies. Raise the outer gate, then lower it again as soon as I'm out.
Those are giants riding mammoths down there. Do you think your cold, rolled steel's gonna stop them?
Tormund. It's over, let it end.

Who's left to give orders? The Wildling army is only an army because of Mance. He united a hundred warring tribes. Without Mance they lose their leader. They lose their purpose, they go back to fighting each other, scatter back to their home.
*grabs Grenn's arm* Hold the gate. If they make it through...
*to Sam* I don't want you out there.
And what did I get for it? An arrow six inches from my heart.
Archers! Knock your arrows! Draw! Loose!
Because I've made that claim.
For one night.
He'll kill me. If I don't go he'll kill me anyway. They'll kill the rest of us too.
I don't think Ser Alliser cares much for interpretation.
I need him more than I need you.
I promised Jeor I'd never lose it again. *he hands the sword to Sam* In case I don't come back.
I'm going to find Mance.
I'm going to try.
Knock! Draw! Loose!
Now!
Oil!
Put him in chains. We'll question him later.
She...she had red hair.
So you and Gilly never-
There will.
They won't summit before dawn.
Two brothers now fight with me.
We can't just let them attack the gate!
We'll go back there.
Well what do you want me to say?
What are you doing up here?
What?
Yes sir.
You're right. It's a bad plan. What's your plan?
He was, your grace.
He's sworn to treat all wounded men, friend or foe.
I know he's the king. My father died for him. My name is Jon Snow, your grace. I'm Ned Stark's son.
I was this man's prisoner once. He could've tortured me, he could've killed me, but he spared my life. I think my father would've taken him prisoner, listened to what he had to say.
No, it's like you said, we don't have the men.
The Halfhand ordered me to join your army and bring back whatever information I could to Castle Black. He made me kill him so you'd trust me. I was loyal, to him and to my Night's Watch vows.
Your grace. If my father'd seen the things that I've seen, he'd also tell you to burn the dead before nightfall. All of them.
Funeral words. I don't know how the Free Folk do it.
Grenn and Mag.
Grenn came from a farm.
He's dead. He killed my friend Grenn.
I don't have a king.
I don't know what happens to the prisoners.
I suppose Stannis does.
I told Tormund and Orell. We have more than a thousand men.
I was sent to discuss terms with the king beyond the wall.
I've been sent to negotiate with you.
No one's going to torture you.
No.
Say farewell.
She put three arrows in me when I escaped.
She told you?
She's dead.
That's not wine.
Turn your army around and go home.
We're gonna burn the bodies of your dead. Do you want to say any words over them?
Yes.
Ygritte.

And what happens to your people? You preserve your dignity and die standing, and they’ll sing songs about you. “You’d rather burn than kneel. The great hero.” Until winter comes, and the white walkers come for us all, and there’s no one left to sing.
Good, good, pivot! Don’t forget to pivot! Shield up!
I want a great many things, your grace. But I’m a sworn brother of the Night’s Watch now.
If it wasn’t heavy it wouldn’t stop a sword, now get it up.

You spent your life convincing 90 clans to come together for the first time in history. Thenns and Hornfoots, the ice-river clans, even the giants. A life’s work uniting them. You didn’t do it for power. You didn’t do it for glory. You brought them together to save them because none of them will survive the winter, not if they’re north of the wall. Isn’t their survival more important than your pride?
You’re not cold, my lady?
Your grace, you want the wildlings to march in your army?
And how many tens of thousands are out there right now? How many women? How many children? And you won’t go out and rescue them because why? You’re afraid of looking afraid.
Come. Try it again. Drive at me. Keep your shield up. Or I’ll ring your head like a bell.
Get your shield up.
Here we are.
How much time do I have?
I don’t think that’s likely.
I respect him.
I think you’re making a terrible mistake.
It’s where she belonged.
No.
Roose Bolton.
Shouldn’t a king be bold?
The men of the Night’s Watch are sworn to play no part--
Then bend the knee and save your people.
They were born on the wrong side of the wall. That doesn’t make them monsters.
They’ll burn you alive.
They’re good men.
This doesn’t have to be our last meeting.
You know what Stannis wants?
You told me you weren’t here to conquer. You told me your people have bled enough.
Your grace…
"Bear Island knows no king but the King in the North, whose name is Stark." (Jon smiles)
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I apologize, Your Grace. Northerners can be a bit like the free folk. Loyal to their own.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I can't. Even if I wanted to, I'm a bastard. A Snow.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I don't doubt it, Your Grace. But I'm a brother of the Night's Watch. I've pledged them my life, my honor, my sword. I don't know what I have left to give you.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I swore a vow to the Night's Watch. If I don't take my own word seriously, what sort of Lord of Winterfell would I be?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: It's the first thing I ever remember wanting. I'd daydream that my father would ask the king, and just like that I would never be the bastard of Winterfell again.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Unpleasant, I expect.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: With respect, Your Grace, the free folk will never follow you, no matter what you do. You're the man who burned their king alive.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I'm going to refuse him.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Most likely.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: No. Only one of their own.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: The Lord Commander's niece.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: I wish I could fight beside you. Believe me, I do. But I swore a sacred vow at the Godswood. I pledged my life to the Night's Watch.
If you have any last words, my Lord, now is the time.
May I ask, Your Grace, how long you plan to stay at Castle Black?
Most of the brothers, yes. There's little love for the free folk here. (Olly stares at him).
Olly is my steward now, as I was Lord Commander Mormont's. I want him to attend my meetings, to learn from men with experience. One day, he might command.
Olly, bring me my sword.

Ser Alliser. (Silence). You have more experience than any other soldier at Castle Black. You proved your valour many times over, while defending the Wall from the wildling attack. I name you First Ranger.

Take good care of him. (To the group). Brothers. As you all know too well, it's long past time to dig a new latrine pit. (Laughter. Jon looks at Alliser). First Builder Yarwyck and I have decided to appoint a latrine captain to oversee this crucial task. (Laughter. After a moment, he continues). Brian. Seems like a good job for a ginger.
Yes, the fort is in a sorry state. Restore it as best you can. First Builder Yarwyck can spare ten of his -
You mistake me, my Lord. That was a command, not an offer. Pack your arms and armour, say your farewells, and ride for Greyguard.
(Stands up). Your Grace.
And the wildlings?
And what needs to be done?
Are you refusing to obey my order?
As long as he's ruling them.
But I have to refuse you. I'm Lord Commander of the Night's Watch. My place is here.
I can imagine no higher praise.
I have. And I thank you for it. You do me great honor. All my life I wanted to be Jon Stark.
I heard it was best to keep your enemies close.
I'm sorry I disappointed him.
I’ve sworn to stay clear of the politics of the Seven Kingdoms.
Lord Janos. I'm giving you command of Greyguard.
Sam. (Sam looks up. Jon looks at Maester Aemon’s chair). Maester Aemon?
Take Lord Janos outside.

You saved us from Mance Rayder's army. We will never forget that. But it's a question of survival. The Night's Watch can't continue to feed your men and the wildling prisoners indefinitely. Winter is coming.
You're gonna show me some vision in the fire. Forgive me, my Lady. But I don't trust in visions.
(To some of the men sparring). Good.
And how many men does this Lord Mazin have to send us?
Castle Black is my home now. Night's Watch takes no part in the wars of the Seven Kingdoms.
He murdered my brother.
How can I help you?
I can't.
I don't think Stannis would like that very much.
I know. But I still love her.
I swore a vow. (Melisandre looks at him incredulously). I loved another.
Never even heard of these people.
Not him.

And how many of your people can't fight? The women, the children, the old, the sick, what happens to them? You're condemning them to death. Worse than death because you're too proud to make peace. Or maybe you're not proud. Maybe you're just a coward.
Because now, (Leans forward), I am Lord Commander of the Night's Watch.
For 8,000 years the Night's Watch have sworn an oath to be the shield that guards the realms of men. And for 8,000 years we've fallen short of that oath. You belong to the realms of men. All of you.
I need your advice. There's something I want to do, something I have to do. But it'll divide the Night's Watch. Bitterly. Half the men will hate me the moment I give the order.
Men, women, and children will die by the thousands if we do nothing.
Sam, I'd like to speak to the maester alone.
Thank you. (Olly walks away). Olly. (He stops). If you have something you want to say to me, say it. It's all right.

You were at the Fist of the First Men. If we abandon them, you know what they become. We can learn to live with the wildlings or we can add them to the army of the dead. Whatever they are now, they're better than that.
You'll get them back, I swear it. Have a safe journey, Your Grace. And thank you.

(Stands up). I'd have you go north of the Wall. Gather the remaining free folk wherever they are and bring them back here. I'll open the gates for them and let them through. I'll find them lands to settle south of the Wall.
And we've slaughtered theirs.
Because you are not my enemy. And I'm not yours.
But you don't know what it is.
How are you feeling?
I don't want them to kneel for me. I want them to fight with me when the time comes.
I know what it's like to lose the people you love. I know this is hard for you. But winter is coming. We know what's coming with it. We can't face it alone.
I'll talk to King Stannis about lending you his fleet.
It is.
It's not a trick.
Maester Aemon.
No.
Up on Storrold's Point. I can give you 10 horses and nine other men. You can get there in a week.
What about you?
What if I unchained you?
Where are the rest of the free folk now? (Tormund scoffs). Where have they gone? (No response). Who leads them?

Your people need a leader. And they need to get south of the Wall before it's too late. We don't have much time and they have less. The walkers are coming and they'll hit your people first. I'm not asking you to make peace to save your skin. Make peace to save your people.
As always, thank you for your honesty.
First Ranger, you have command of Castle Black.
Thank you, Sam.
Me too.
I knew Mance Rayder. He never wanted a war with the Night’s Watch, he wanted a new life for his people. For you. We’re prepared to give you that new life.

I’m not asking you to forget your dead. I’ll never forget mine. I lost 50 brothers the night that Mance attacked the wall. But I’m asking you to think about your children now. They’ll never have children of their own if we don’t band together. The Long Night is coming and the dead come with it. No clan can stop them. The free folk can’t stop them. The Night’s Watch can’t stop them. And all the southern kings can’t stop them. Only together, all of us, and even then it might not be enough, but at least then we’ll give the fuckers a fight.
And he knew them better than I ever will.
Come with me and I'll share these weapons.
Does that make me a fool?
Dragonglass. A man of the Night’s Watch used one of these daggers to kill a walker.
Get in line! Get in line!
Get them to the ship and come back for me!
Go! Go! Go!
He died.
He’s not my prisoner.
Hold the line! Hold the line!
How many are with us? 5000?
I put an arrow through his heart.
If you swear you join us when the real war begins.
In normal times we wouldn’t. But these aren’t normal times. The white walkers don’t care if a man’s Free Folk or Crow. We’re all the same to them. Meat to their army. But together we can beat them.
It’s not a trick. It’s a gift for those who join us.
Let’s go now! Now!

My name’s Jon Snow. I’m Lord Commander of the Night’s Watch. We’re not friends. We’ve never been friends. We won’t become friends today. This isn’t about friendship. This is about survival. This is about putting a 700 foot wall between you and what’s out there.
Night’s Watch with me! Move! Move! Move!
No. But I trust the man.
Now!
The Dragonglass!
The Dragonglass!
There are good lands south of the wall.  The Night's Watch will allow you through the tunnel and allow your people to farm those lands.
We’re allies.
We’re leaving too many behind.
We’re not here to fight. We’re here to talk.
You have my word. I’ve given orders.
You should be on one of those boats.
I went to save them, I failed.
I don’t think that fact’s lost on them.
It was a failure.
He raised his hands. And they all stood up at once. Tens of thousands of them, the biggest army in the world.
I need you here, Sam. If you leave, who’s left to give me advice I trust?
No one’s ever getting that back, now. It wouldn’t have mattered anyway, unless we had a mountain of it.
The wildlings won’t ever fight for Stannis, I told you before--
You know that The Citadel will make you swear off women, too.
Are you sure he’s talking about Benjen?
He could be lying.
Here’s to us then. Long may they sneer.
I’m glad the end of the world’s working out for someone.
I’m gonna hope they don’t learn how to climb the wall.
It’s not their fight!

Not enough. (pause) The first Lord Commander in history to sacrifice the lives of sworn Brothers to save the lives of Wildlings. How’s it feel to be friends with the most hated man in Castle Black? (smiles)
Olly…
Sam?
Sam…
Stannis?
To your return.
We don’t have enough men to make any difference.
What?
Where is he?
With Longclaw. I saw them shatter steel axes like they were glass. But Longclaw…
You’ve just been beaten half to death. How did you--
I don’t know how to do that. I thought I did, but...I failed.
I’m sure you would, Ser Alliser.
If you have any last words, now is the time.
I did what I thought was right. And I got murdered for it. And now I’m back. Why?
I think so. Hold off on burning my body for now.
I’m not a god.
My watch is ended.
Neither was killing me.
Nothing. There was nothing at all.
They stabbed me. Olly...he put a knife in my heart. I shouldn’t be here.
Wear it. Burn it. Whatever you want. You have Castle Black.
You should.

"To the traitor and bastard Jon Snow. You allowed thousands of wildlings past the Wall. You have betrayed your own kind. You have betrayed the North. Winterfell is mine, bastard. Come and see. Your brother Rickon is in my dungeon.
All right. All right, I forgive you.
Aye, I pledged my life to the Night’s Watch. I gave my life.

His direwolf's skin is on my floor. Come and see. I want my bride back. Send her to me, bastard, and I will not trouble you or your wildling lovers. Keep her from me and I will ride north and slaughter every wildling man, woman, and babe living under your protection. You will watch as I skin them living. You --
I can’t stay here, not after what happened.
They killed me, Edd! My own brothers. You want me to stay here after that?
Where will we go? If I don’t watch over you, Father’s ghost will come back and murder me.
You’d think after thousands of years, the Night’s Watch would have learned how to make a good ale.
Get warm.
How could we know?
How many do you have?
I did everything I could. You know that.
I don’t have an army.
I’m not Lord Commander anymore.
I’m tired of fighting.
It’s all I’ve done since I left home. I’ve killed brothers of the Night’s Watch. I’ve killed wildlings. I’ve killed men that I admire. I hanged a boy younger than Bran. I fought and I lost.
It's just more of the same.
Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North.
Sansa.
Should we tell the Boltons to pack up and leave?
South.
There’s nothing to forgive.
They didn’t come here to serve me.
We don’t know that.
We never should have left Winterfell.
We were children.
With the peas and onions?
You were occasionally awful. I’m sure I can’t have been great fun. Always sulking in the corner while the rest of you played.
Thank you, Sansa.
We can’t defend the north from the Walkers and the south from the Boltons. If we want to survive, we need Winterfell, and to take Winterfell, we need more men.
Don’t knock it down while I’m gone.
How do you know that?
New dress?
Lord Glover, if you could just hear us out.
Robb is gone, but House Stark is not. And it needs your support now more than ever. I’ve come with my sister to ask for House Mormont’s allegiance.
Thank you, my lady. How many fighting men can we expect?

That’s what’ll happen to you if we lose. The Boltons, the Karstarks, the Umbers, they know you’re here. They know that more than half of you are women and children. After they finish with me, they’ll come for you. You’re right. This isn’t your fight. You shouldn’t have to come to Winterfell with me. I shouldn’t be asking you. It’s not the deal we made. I need you with me if we’re gonna beat them, and we need to beat them if you’re going to survive.
Are you sure they’ll come?
House Mormont.
Lady Mormont.
Lord Glover.
Ser Davos is the reason I’m standing here talking to you and he served Stannis for years.
The bulk of the force is made up of wildlings.
There’s no time.
We fight with the army we have.
We have to march on Winterfell now while we still can.
We’re not staying here long. Another storm could hit any day..
Your uncle fought them at the Fist of the First Men. I fought them at Hardhome. We both lost.
All right, tell me. What should we do? How do we get Rickon back?
Aye, and what have I been doing all my life? Playing with broomsticks? I fought beyond the Wall against worse than Ramsay Bolton. I’ve defended the Wall from worse than Ramsay Bolton.
Aye, for what they’re worth.
Aye, that’s good advice.
Aye, you have the numbers. Will your men want to fight for you when they hear you wouldn’t fight for them?
If he was smart, he’d stay inside the walls of Winterfell and wait us out.
If I do, if I fall, don’t bring me back.
It’s his weakness, too. His mean don’t want to fight for him. They’re forced to fight for him. If they feel the tide turning...
Now, it’s not enough! It’s what we have! Battles have been won against greater odds.
We’re digging trenches all along our flanks. They won’t be able to hit us the way that Stannis hit you, in a double envelopment.
Well, it is a bit obvious.
A pincer move.
Any advice?
Get word --
He’s overconfident.
How do you know what he commands?
I’m ordering you not to bring me back.
My lady. You weren’t at the war council.
No. But I wanted to make him angry. I want him coming at us full tilt.
Of course he does. What should I do differently?
They won’t be able to hit us from the sides.
We can’t give up on our brother.
We’re going to bury my brother in the crypt next to my father.
What kind of god would do something like that?
When will we have a larger force?! We’ve pleaded with every house that’ll have us! The Blackfish can’t help us! We’re lucky to have this many men!
Why?
You don’t have to be here.
You’re in my camp. I’m the commander.
You’re right.
You’re right. There’s no need for a battle. Thousands of men don’t need to die. Only one of us. Let’s end this the old way. You against me.
Aye, you’re right. I was luckier than most.
Ride south today. If you return to the North, I’ll have you hanged as a murderer.
The free folk, the northerners, and the Knights of the Vale fought bravely, fought together, and we won. My father uses to say we find our true friends on the battlefield.
The war is not over. And I promise you, friend, the true enemy won’t wait out the storm. He brings the storm.
There’s nothing to forgive, my lord.
Well, Father always promised, didn’t he?
When we had feasts, our family would sit up here… and I’d sit down there.
And you trust him?
Do you have anything to say for yourself?
I’m having the lord’s chamber prepared for you.
I’m not a Stark.
We need to trust each other. We can’t fight a war amongst ourselves. We have so many enemies now.
What is that?

You’re the Lady of Winterfell. You deserve it. We’re standing here because of you. The battle was lost until the Knights of the Vale rode in. They came because of you. You told me Lord Baelish sold you to the Boltons.
"Cersei of House Lannister, First of Her Name, Queen of the Andals and the First Men, Protector of the Seven Kingdoms--"
All right, I'll stop trying to protect you and you stop trying to undermine me.
For centuries, our families fought side by side on the battlefield. I ask you to pledge your loyalty once again to House Stark, to serve as our bannermen and come to our aid whenever called upon.

I want every northern maester to scour their records for any mention of dragonglass. Dragonglass kills white walkers. It's more valuable to us now than gold. We need to find it, we need to mine it, we need to make weapons from it. Everyone aged 10 to 60 will drill daily with spears, pikes, bow and arrow.
I'm consumed with the Night King because I've seen him. And believe me, you'd think of little else if you had, too.
If they breach the wall, the first two castles in their path are Last Hearth and Karhold.
No. No, he never cursed in front of his girls.
Of course you can, but--
What did father used to say? Everything before the word "but" is horse shit.

When I was Lord Commander of the Night's Watch I executed men who betrayed me. I executed men who refused to follow orders. My father always said, "The man who passes the sentence should swing the sword," and I have tried to live by those words. But I will not punish a son for his father's sins, and I will not take a family home away from a family it has belonged to for centuries. That is my decision, and my decision is final.
When you question my decisions in front of the other lords and ladies, you undermine me.

While we're preparing for attack, we need to shore up our defenses. The only thing standing between us and the Army of the Dead is the Wall and the Wall hasn't been properly manned in centuries. I'm not the king of the Free Folk.
Yesterday's wars don't matter anymore. The North needs to band together, all the living north. Will you stand beside me, Ned and Alys, now and always?
You are my sister, but I am king now.
Alys Karstark.
And how should I be smarter? By listening to you?
At what?
But if we're going to survive this winter together...
Come to King's Landing. Bend the knee or suffer the fate of all traitors.
Do you think I'm Joffrey?
I'm not going to strip these families of their ancestral homes because of the crimes of a few reckless sons.
Last time we saw the Night King was at Hardhome. The closest castle to Hardhome is Eastwatch-by-the-Sea.
Ned Umber.
No.
Not just the boys.
Stand.
Thank you.
The punishment for treason is death. Smalljon Umber died on the field of battle. Harald Karstark died on the field of battle.
The Umbers and the Karstarks have fought beside the Starks for centuries. They've kept faith for generation after generation.
There's a thousand miles between us and Cersei. Winter is here. The Lannisters are a southern army. They've never ranged this far north.
We can't defend the North if only half the population is fighting.
You almost sound as if you admire her.

But more importantly, we need allies.  The Night King's army grows larger by the day.  We can't defeat them on our own.  We don't have the numbers.  Daenerys has her own army and she has dragon fire.  I need to try and persuade her to fight with us.  Ser Davos and I will ride for White Harbor tomorrow, then sail for Dragonstone.

He's now Hand of the Queen to Daenerys Targaryen.  She intends to take the Iron Throne from Cersei Lannister.  She has a powerful army at her back and if this message is to be believed, three dragons.
It could be, but I don't believe Tyrion would do that.  You know him.  He's a good man.
We need this Dragonglass, My Lords.  We know that Dragonglass can destroy both White Walkers and their army.  We need to mine it and turn it into weapons.

You all crowned me your king.  I never wanted it, I never asked for it, but I accepted it because the north is my home.  It's part of me and I will never stop fighting for it, no matter the odds.  But the odds are against us.  None of you have seen the Army of the Dead.  None of you.  We can never hope to defeat them alone.  We need allies, powerful allies.  I know it's a risk.  But I have to take it.
Yours.  You are my sister.  You are the only Stark in Winterfell.  Until I return, the north is yours.
But?
Come in.
Daenerys is a queen.  Only a king can convince her to help us.  It has to be me.
I have nothing to say to you.
I know that.
I received this a few days ago from Dragonstone.  It was sent to me by Tyrion Lannister.
I'm leaving both in good hands.
It's something he said to me the first night we met.  You know him better than any of us.  What do you think?
Lord Tyrion has invited me to Dragonstone to meet with Daenerys and I'm going to accept.
Read the last bit.

This message was sent to me by Samwell Tarley.  He was my brother at the Night's Watch.  A man I trust as much as anyone in this world.  He's discovered proof that Dragonstone sits on a mountain of Dragonglass.
Touch my sister and I'll kill you myself.
What?
You don't belong down here.
Because right now you and I and Cersei and everyone else, we're children playing at a game screaming that the rules aren't fair.
Everyone told me to learn from my father's mistakes.  Don't go south.  Don't answer a summons from the Mad King's daughter, a foreign invader.  And here I am.  A northern fool.

I mean no offense, Your Grace, but I don't know you.  As far as I can tell your claim to the throne rests entirely on your father's name.  And my own father fought to overthrow the Mad King.  The lords of the north placed their trust in me to lead them.  And I will continue to do so as well as I can.
The winds were kind, Your Grace.
You don't know me well, My Lord, but do you think I am a liar or a madman?
You were pissing off the edge, if I remember right.  You picked up some scars along the road.
Your Grace, everone you know will die before winter is over if we don't defeat the enemy to the north.
Am I your prisoner?
Amazing thing to see.
And why would I do that?
As long as you tell me how a Lannister became Hand to Daenerys Targaryen.
Because I need your help and you need mine.
Break faith?  Your father burned my grandfather alive.  He burned my uncle alive.  He would have burned the Seven Kingdoms.

But you haven't stormed King's Landing.  Why not?  The only reason I can see is you don't want to kill thousands of innocent people.  It's the fastest way to win the war but you won't do it.  Which means at the very least you're better than Cersei.
Except to my ship.  They took my ship.
He enjoys talking.
How do I convince people who don't know me that an enemey they don't believe in is coming to kill them all?
I am not your enemy.  The dead are the enemy.
I am not.
I did.
I didn't ask --
I don't.
I haven't either.
I know it's a good question.  I'm looking for an answer.
I need to help prepare my people for what's coming.  I can't help them from here.  I'd like to leave.
I never expected that you would.
I'm a prisoner on this island.
I'm not playing word games with you.  The dead are coming for us all.
It's hard for me to fathom.  It really is.  If someone told me about the White Walkers and the Night King --
My bannermen think I'm a fool for coming here.
Of course.
She is.
She's starting to let on.
So you believe me then about the Night King and the Army of the Dead?
Thank you.
The Army of the Dead is on the march.
The Army of the Dead is real.  The White Walkers are real.  The Knight King is real.  I've seen them.  If they get past the wall and we're squabbling amongst ourselves --
The dwarf of Casterly Rock.
There's no time for that.  There's no time for any of this.  While we stand here debating --
They're hard to miss.
True.  But I'm not a Stark.
What do you mean?
You didn't before.  Grumpkins and snarks you called them.  Do you remember?  You said it was all nonsense.
You probably don't believe me.
You'll be ruling over a graveyard if we don't defeat the Night King.
You're right.  You're not guilty of your father's crime.  And I'm not beholden to my ancestor's vows.
You've been talking to Tyrion?
But if you use them to melt castles and burn cities, you're not different.  You're just more of the same.
My people won't accept a southern ruler, not after everything they've suffered.
No, I don't think I can.
So this is it, all we'll ever need.
There is something else I want to show your, Your Grace.
There's no time for that.  I saw the Night King, Davos.  I looked into his eyes.  How many men do we have in the north to fight him?  10,000?  Less?
A very long time ago.
And if you wanted to sail home to Naarth tomorrow --
Because the enemy is real.  It's always been real.

I never thought that dragons would exist again.  No one did.  The people who follow you know that you made something impossible happen.  Maybe that helps them believe that you can make other impossible things happen.  Build a world that's different from the shit one they've always known.
I think she has a good heart.
I wanted you to see it before we start hacking it to bits.
I would never presume to --
I'm a bastard.  My mother and father weren't married.
I'm sorry.
Is that a Greyjoy ship?
It doesn't matter.
No.
Of course.
The Children of the Forest made these.
The queen is gone.
They were here together the Children and the First Men.
What you did for her is the only reason I'm not killing you.
What?
Who?
Why did you leave your homeland?
You believe that?
Your Grace.
I wish you good fortune in the wars to come, Your Grace.
No, I'm not.
That wasn't the word I was thinking of, but, yes, they are.  Gorgeous beasts.
Well, that's a problem.

With respect, Your Grace, I don't need your permission.  I am a king.  And I came here knowing that you could have your men behead me or your dragons burn me alive.  I put my trust in you, a stranger, because I knew it was the best chance for my people, for all our people.  Now I'm asking you to trust in a stranger because it's our best chance.
Your Grace, this is --
And?
Both.
Bran saw the Night King and his army marching towards Eastwatch.  If they make it past the wall --
Do you know how to use one?
He's right.  We're all on the same side.
Heave!
I don't think she'll come see the dead at my invitation.
I grew up on stories about them.
I need to go home.
I saw your father once at Winterfell.
I served with your father.  He was a great man.
I thought Arya was dead.  I thought Bran was dead.
I'm the only one here whose fought them.  I'm the only one here who knows them.
If I don't return at least you won't have to deal with the King in the North anymore.
Not enough.
Ser Davos gets carried away.
The first White I ever saw was brought into Castle Black from beyond the Wall.
The free folk will help us.  They know the real north better than anyone.
They won't have to.
Thousands.
Two.
We could use the help.
We'll fight with the men we have.  Unless you'll join us.
We're all breathing.
You survived King's Landing.
You weren't gone long.
You're a lot leaner.
You're The Hound.  I saw you once at Winterfell.
"I am the shield that guards the realms of men."
That's all anyone can tell me, I don't know.  So what's the point in serving a god that none of us knows what he wants.
Your father gave me this sword.  He changed the pommel from a bear to a wolf, but it's still Long Claw.
Are you all right?
Aye.  Maybe that's enough.
But we all die.
But you are back and it's been in your family for centuries.  It's not right for me to have it.
Davos says he is a strong fighter.
Ever been north before?
I hate that he died that way.  My father was the most honorable man I ever met.  He was good all the way through.  And he died on the executioner's block.
I heard.
I serve the north.
I was a prisoner of the Wildlings.  But we avenged him.  I want you to know that every mutineer found justice.
I'm glad he didn't capture you.
I'm not his son.
Lord Commander Mormont thought you'd never come back to Westeros.
She'll only fight beside us if I bend the knee.
So what are you fighting for?
That's the north.
The first time I went north of the wall was with your father.
The lord of light never spoke to me.  I don't know anything about him.  I don't know what he wants from me.
There's not another woman within 100 miles of here.
Who's that?
Why?
You knew him?
You've never been down south.
And we can destroy them with dragonglass. If we don't win this fight, then that is the fate of every person in the world.
If we have the Dothraki ride hard on the kingsroad, they'll arrive at Winterfell within the fortnight.
It's not my place to forgive you for all of it. But what I can forgive, I do. You don't need to choose. You're a Greyjoy and you're a Stark.
It's not. It may seem that way from the outside, but I promise you, it's not true. I've done plenty of things that I regret.

It's your decision, Your Grace. But if we're going to be allies in this war, it's important for the Northerners to see us as allies. If we sail to White Harbor together, I think it sends a better message.
So, what now?
So, why are you still talking to me?
(SIGHS)
(SIGHS) But you never lost him. He's a part of you. Just like he's a part of me.
All right.
And you betrayed him. Betrayed his memory.
Has it occurred to you she might not have been a reliable source of information?
How many people live here?
I am true to my word. Or I try to be. That is why I cannot give you what you ask. I cannot serve two queens. And I have already pledged myself to Queen Daenerys of House Targaryen.
I did this. I should go.
I know!
I'm not going to swear an oath I can't uphold. Talk about my father.
It appears Tyrion's assessment was correct. We're fucked.
No one is less happy about this than I am.
No.
No. Not compared to you.
Our father was more of a father.
That's more people than the entire North crammed into that. Why would anyone want to live that way?
There is only one war that matters the Great War. And it is here.
This is serious. I wouldn't be here if it weren't.

This isn't about living in harmony. It's just about living. The same thing is coming for all of us. A general you can't negotiate with. An army that doesn't leave corpses behind on the battlefield. Lord Tyrion tells me a million people live in this city. They're about to become a million more soldiers in the Army of the Dead.
We can destroy them by burning them.
We went down there to make peace. And it seems to me we need to be honest with each other if we're going to fight together.
Who told you that?
You're not like everyone else. And your family hasn't seen its end. You're still here.
"House Glover will stand behind House Stark as we have for a thousand years." Isn't that what he said?

Do you think we can beat the Army of the Dead without her? I fought them, Sansa. Twice. You want to worry about who holds what title, I'm telling you it doesn't matter. Without her, we don't stand a chance.
I gave up my crown, Sam.
I never wanted a crown. All I wanted was to protect the North. I brought two armies home with me, two dragons.
Queen Daenerys of House Targaryen. My sister, Sansa Stark, the Lady of Winterfell.
She doesn't know you. If it makes you feel any better, she didn't like me either when we were growing up.
Well, I've executed men who disobeyed me.

You did, my lady. It was the honor of my life. I’ll always be grateful for your faith. But when I left Winterfell, I told you we need allies or we will die. I have brought those allies home to fight alongside us. I had a choice, keep my crown or protect the North. I chose the North.
Daenerys is our queen.
Do you have any faith in me at all?
Have you ever used it?
How did you sneak up on me?
I bent the knee. I'm not King in the North anymore.
I didn't.
I don't know how to ride a dragon.
I told you we needed allies.
I warned you. Northerners don't much trust outsiders.
I wasn't a king.
I'm so sorry. We need to end this war.
It's cold up here for a southern girl.
It's not important.
Jealous?
Know what?
Little Sam?
Look at you.
My father was the most honorable man I ever met. You're saying he lied to me all my life.
Now you're defending her?
Sam?
Sansa thinks she's smarter than everyone.
She'll be a good queen. For all of us. She's not her father.
That's treason.
We need to send ravens to the Night's Watch as well. There's no sense in manning the castles anymore. We make our stand here.
We'd be pretty old.
Were you hiding from me?
What are you doing in Winterfell? Or did you read every book in the Citadel already?
What are you talking about?
What do I hold onto?
What if he doesn't want me to?
What's the matter?
What's wrong with them?
What's wrong? Gilly? Is she all right?
Where were you before? I could've used your help with Sansa.
Where's Arya?
Yeah. I'm her family too.
You still have it.
You?
You're a man.
You've completely ruined horses for me.
If they're in the field, they're not protecting Bran. We need to be near him. Not too near, or the Night King won't come. But close enough to pursue him when he does.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: It's true, Dany. I know it is.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: They were married in secret. After Rhaegar fell on the Trident, she had a son. Robert would have murdered the baby if he ever found out, and Lyanna knew it. So the last thing she did, as she bled to death on her birthing bed, was give the boy to her brother, Ned Stark, to raise as his bastard.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: We'll put you in the crypt, where it's safest.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: And Samwell confirmed it. He read about their marriage at the Citadel without even knowing what it meant.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Bran. He saw it.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: But there are too many of them.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Doesn't stop. Doesn't feel.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Far too many.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Gilly? Little Sam?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: He didn't. He loved her.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: How did you find each other?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: How long do we have?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I thought we'd lost you.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I wish it were.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: If you want to join them.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Let's get some rest.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Lyanna Stark.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: My name. My real name is Aegon Targaryen.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: No.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Now it's just us three.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Our enemy doesn't tire.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: The Night King made them all. They follow his command. If he falls Getting to him may be our best chance.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: The Umbers?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: They're coming.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: To protect them.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: We can't beat them in a straight fight.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: We have dragonglass and Valyrian steel.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: We need every man we can get.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Your Grace.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: No, not in one go.
I didn't know Ser Jorah well but I know this. If he could have chosen a way to die, it would have been protecting you.
I wish I was going with you. This is farewell, then.
If you only trust the people you grew up with, you won't make many allies.
This is the North, you know. And the Free Folk are welcome to stay.
We'll surround the city. If the Iron Fleet tries to ferry in more food, the dragons will destroy them. If the Lannisters and the Golden Company attack, we'll defeat them in the field.
What you command, we will obey.
Yeah, I didn't have much say in that.
You have to swear it, before I tell you.
You too, Sam.
(laughs)
(to BRAN) Tell them.
(to SANSA and ARYA) The Northern forces will honor their promises and their allegiance to the Queen of the Seven Kingdoms.
Arya -
Because we're family. Swear it.
Her men gave their lives defending Winterfell--
I have to tell Sansa and Arya.
I hope it's a girl.
I need to tell you something. But you have to swear you'll never tell another soul.
I owe them the truth.
I swore myself and the North to her cause.
I told you I don't want it.
I'll refuse. You are my queen. I don't know what else I can say.
I've never been a Stark.
It won't.
It's where he belongs too. A direwolf has no place in the south. Will you take him with you? He'll be happier up there.
Just a horse. Rhaegal needs to heal. He doesn't need me weighing him down.
No.
Only a little.
She won't.
The Northmen as well.
Vomiting is not celebrating.

We're here to say goodbye to our  brothers and sisters. To our fathers and mothers. To our friends. Our fellow men and women who set aside their differences to fight together and die together so that others might live. Everyone in this world owes them a debt that can never be repaid. It is our duty and our honor to keep them alive in memory for those who come after us and those who come after them for as long as men draw breath. They were the shields that guarded the realms of men. And we shall never see their like again.
You are my queen. Nothing will change that. And they are my family. We can live together.
You don't know her yet.
You understand we'd all be dead if not for her. We'd be corpses marching down to King's Landing.
Necessary? Have you been down there? Have you seen? Children, little children, burned!
"Love is the death of duty."
Ah, it's easy to judge when you're standing far from the battlefield.
And you've been by her side, counseling her. Until today.
Hey, what happened?
Please, Dany.
The North is free, thanks to you.
You are my queen. Now, and always.
You can come see me, you know, at Castle Black.
You can. You can forgive all of them, make them see they made a mistake. Make them understand.
You think our house words are stamped on our bodies when we're born and that's who we are? Then I'd be fire and blood too. She's not her father, no more than you're Tywin Lannister.
(chuckles) I don't know.
(nods)
(sighs)
(sighs)
(sighs)
(sighs) She's everyone's queen now.
And Tyrion?
And what are the queen's commands?
Cersei left her no choice.
Forgive him.
Grey Worm!
How do you know? How do you know it'll be good?
I can't justify what happened. I won't try. But the war is over now.
I don't expect we'll ever see each other again.
I don't know.
I don't.
I saw them executing Lannister prisoners in the street. They said they were acting on your orders.
I'm sorry it came to this.
It doesn't feel right.
It's not safe. Let me send some men with you.
It's over.
Maester Aemon said it a long time ago.
My sisters will be loyal to the throne.
Ned Stark's daughter will speak for them. She's the best they could ask for.
No.
Not that I've seen.
She doesn't get to choose.
She saw her friend beheaded. She saw her dragon shot out of the sky.
That's her decision. She is the queen.
The world we need is a world of mercy. It has to be.
There's still a Night's Watch?
Wait for me outside the city gates. I'll come find you.
Was it right? What I did?
What about everyone else? All the other people who think they know what's good.
What are you doing here?
What?
What's it matter what I'd do?
You didn't.
You have your Needle?
You think anyone will dare tell you women aren't allowed?
Your Grace. I'm sorry I wasn't there when you needed me.
You cannot mean to hold to Joffrey, my lord. He put your father to death.
Do you mean to declare us for Stannis?
Ser Jorah Mormont of Bear Island. I served your father for many years. Gods be good, I hope to always serve the rightful king.
A small gift for the new Khaleesi. Songs and histories from the Seven Kingdoms.
There is no word for ‘thank you’ in Dothraki.
As you wish, your Grace.

The Dothraki have two things in abundance: grass and horses. People can't live on grass... In the Shadow Lands beyond Asshai, they say there are fields of ghost grass with stalks as pale as milk that glow in the night. It murders all other grass. The Dothraki believe that one day it will cover everything. That's the way the world will end… It'll get easier.
We're still not far from Pentos, your Grace. Magister Illyrio has extended his hospitality. You'd be more comfortable there.
You need to drink, child... And eat.
Selling to one... Some poachers I caught on my land.

For a man on horseback, the curved blade is a good thing, easier to handle. It's a good weapon for a Dothrakan. But a man in full plate... the arakh won't get through the steel. That's where the broadsword has the advantage. Designed for piercing plate.
