If you rule a city and you see the horde approaching, you have two choices : pay tribute or fight. An easy choice for most. Of course, sometimes it's not enough. Sometimes a Khal feels insulted by the number of slaves he's given. He might think the men too weak or the women too ugly. Sometimes a Khal decides his riders haven't had a good fight in months and need the practice.
Shall we return to the Khalasar, Khaleesi?
It's true, but it also keeps a man alive.
Armor.
Don't worry. I'll catch you. The horde's easy to find.
He still is. A man of great honor. And I betrayed him.
I don't think she wants to eat dog.
I'll have the boys butcher a goat for supper. I need to ride to Qohor.
I’ve heard that your father was a famous warrior.
Slow.
The Dothraki don't believe in money. Most of their slaves were given to them as gifts.
You want the entire horde to stop? For how long?
You're learning to talk like a Queen.
I fought beside them once, long ago. Now Ned Stark wants my head. He drove me from my land.
In another place, with another man.
King Robert is fool enough to meet them in open battle, but the men advising him are different.
The common people pray for rain, health and a summer that never ends. They don't care what games the high Lords play.
Aye.
Home.
I had no money and an expensive wife.
The Dothraki have never crossed the Narrow Sea. They fear any water their horses can't drink.
The truth now: do you want to see your brother sitting on the Iron Throne?
Vaes Dothrak. The city of the horselords.
Your brother Rhaegar was the last dragon. Viserys is less than the shadow of a snake.
“The stallion who mounts the world.” The stallion, is the khal of khals. He shall unite the people into a single khalasar. All the people of the world will be his herd.
Once, perhaps.
You can go, but you can’t have the eggs.
“The prince is riding. I have heard the thunder of the hooves. Swift as the wind he rides. His enemies will cower before him… and their wives will weep tears of blood.”
And here I stand.
And you have all three.
Come.
Don’t let them see you carrying a sword in Vaes Dothrak. You know the law.
It means everything to me.
Khal Drogo says there is a place for you. Back there.
Khaleesi…
Look away Khaleesi.
Put the sword down. They’ll kill us all.
She truly is a queen today.
She’s doing well.
She’s going to have a boy.
They don’t belong to you.
Ah well, having a few dragons makes things easier.
Forgive me, Khaleesi, but your ancestor Aegon the Conqueror didn't seize six of the kingdoms because they were his right.
Have you ever seen a dragon, Khaleesi? I believe what my eyes and ears report.
No no, don't trouble yourself. Enjoy the market. I'll rejoin you soon enough.
The Dothraki do things in their own time, for their own reasons.
Come.
I have a thirst. Open it.
Open it.
Pour.
Put down that cask.
Rakharo.
You first.
Gold to hire ships, Princess. Ships to sail to Westeros.
Princess, you have a gentle heart, but this is how it's always been.
You cannot claim them all, Princess.
Come.
Khaleesi?
These men have shed blood for their Khal.
What do you want done with them?
(in Dothraki) No further, horselord.
He will die tonight, Khaleesi.
He's already gone, Khaleesi.

This isn't Westeros, where men honor blood. Here they only honor strength. There will be fighting after Drogo dies. Whoever wins that fight will be the new khal. He won't want any rivals. Your boy will be plucked from your breast and given to the dogs.
(in Dothraki) Rein in your tongue. She is still your Khaleesi.
Are you hurt?
Even a queen doesn't have that power. We must go quickly. l've heard there's a good port in Asshai.
Fetch the midwives.
Hmm?
Khaleesi.
l think you're right.
They'll come or l'll have their heads.
A khal who cannot ride is no khal. The Dothraki follow only the strong. l'm sorry, my Princess.
Gently, gently.
He never lived, my princess. The women say –
He seems to like the warmth, Princess.
Khaleesi, my Queen, l vow to serve you, obey you, to die for you if need be, but let him go, Khaleesi. l know what you intend. Do not.
(bowing)  Blood of my blood.
Don't ask me to stand aside as you climb on that pyre. l won't watch you burn.
Drogo will have no use for dragon eggs in the Night Lands. Sell them. You can return to the Free Cities and live as a wealthy woman for all your days.
l don't want you alone with this sorceress.
The boy did not live.
They say the child was –
Time enough for that later –
What is there to tell?
A trick I never learned, I'm afraid.
If we go south to the land of the Lazarheen, the Lamb Men will kill us and take your dragons. If we go west to the Dothraki Sea, the first Khalasar we meet will kill us and take your dragons.
They are too weak to fight, as are your people. You must be their strength.
This is further east than I've ever been. But, yes, Khaleesi, everything ends, even the red waste.
I remember.
Everything ends, even the red waste.
Khal Pono perhaps, Khal Jhaqo They don't like the idea of a woman leading a Khalasaar.
Khaleesi.
You don't need to see this.
If you heard a Dothraki horde was approaching your city, you might do the same, Khaleesi.
Khaleesi, please be careful.
Only that the desert around their walls is called the Garden of Bones. Every time the Qartheen shut their gates on a traveler, the garden grows.
Make your own way. Find your own ship. You only need one. The allies we need are in Westeros, not Qarth.
Malakko says the statue is too heavy to carry. Kovarro says that Malakko is an idiot. They can pry out the gems, the rest is pure gold. Very soft. He can chop off as much as we can carry.
Rich men do not become rich by giving more than they get. They'll give you ships and soldiers and they'll own you forever. Moving carefully is the hard way, but it's the right way.
To win Westeros, you need support from Westeros.

You have a good claim. A title. A birthright. But you have something more than that. You may cover it up and deny it, but you have a gentle heart. You would not only be respected and feared, you would be loved. Someone who can rule and should rule. Centuries come and go without a person like that coming into the world. There are times when I look at you and I still can't believe you're real.
(laughs)
(speakss Dothraki)
According to your new friend who earned your trust by cutting his hand?
Do I know you?
I only want
I'll find it for you. A sound ship with a good captain.
If you cross the sea with an army you bought and a foreign husband who paid for it . . .
It's not the only thing. They're quite good at killing the better men.
Khaleesi.
To see you on the Iron Throne.
Who are you?
Forgive me, Khaleesi. No one can survive in this world without help. No one. Let me help you, please. Tell me how.
Trust me, Khaleesi.
As soon as I heard. Do you know anything?
Doreah?
I didn't come here for lessons.
I know. She was a good
I should have been here.
My place is by your side. I shouldn't have left you alone with these people.
Never.
They are not to be trusted.
They will soon enough.
Where are the dragons?
You are too young to be so
You have them? Where are they?
Your people are in Westeros.
Careful, Khaleesi. Xaro owns this city and the warlocks have a thousand eyes watching for you. I found one. A ship with a good captain. She leaves for Astapor tomorrow.
That's what the warlock wants. He told you so himself. If you enter that place, you will never leave again. His magic is strong.

They're not your children. I know they call you the Mother of Dragons and I know you love them, but you didn't grow them in your womb. They didn't suckle at your breast. They are dragons, Khaleesi. And if we stay in Qarth, we'll die.
Until my last breath, I will remember. After I have forgotten my mother's face.
You know I would die for you. I will never abandon you. I'm sworn to protect you, to serve.
(nods)
We cannot stay here.
Aye, a small ship.
No guards. The warlocks kill with sorcery, not steel.
(dothraki) Take all the gold and jewels.
It's all a lie.
Khaleesi! Khaleesi!
The Dothraki follow strength above all, khaleesi. You'll have a true khalasar when you prove yourself strong. And not before.
(Nods.) I know him as one of the greatest fighters the Seven Kingdoms has ever seen and as the Lord Commander of Robert Baratheon's Kingsguard.
Do those people have any better ideas about how to put you on the Iron Throne?
Do you think these slaves will have better lives serving Kraznys and men like him or serving you?
Even the bravest men fear death.
The Unsullied are a means to an end.
They're growing fast.
They're not men. Not anymore.
We'll be in Astapor by nightfall. Some say the Unsullied are the greatest soldiers in the world.
You won't order them to murder babies. You'll see they're properly fed and sheltered. A great injustice has been done to them. Closing your eyes will not undo it.
You'll be fair to them. You won't mutilate them to make a point.
You're right. Another lovely day on the high seas.
How many wars have you fought in, Ser Barristan?

I was in King's Landing after the sack, khaleesi. You know what I saw? Butchery. Babies, children, old men. More women raped than you can count. There's a beast in every man and it stirs when you put a sword in his hand. But the Unsullied are not men. They do not rape. They do not put cities to the sword unless they're ordered to do so. If you buy them, the only men they'll kill are those you want dead.
Is it 'we' already, Ser Barristan? If you want to sit on the throne your ancestors built, you must win it. That will mean blood on your hands before the thing is done.
Khaleesi, a dragon is worth more than any army.
Khaleesi, please.
Khaleesi, this man has been sentenced to death.
Rhaegar fought valiantly, Rhaegar fought nobly, and Rhaegar died.
And what is she to do for soldiers?
Have you ever seen a war where innocents didn't die by the thousands?
Our cause? Forgive me, Ser Barristan, but I was busy defending the khaleesi against King Robert's assassins while you were still bowing to the man.

Proudest moment of my life. One knee in the dust, the king's sword on my shoulder, listening to the words. 'In the name of the Warrior, I charge you to be brave.' All I could think of was how badly I had to piss.
Still, she'll have to wade through that muck if she wants to rule the Seven Kingdoms.
The second. Thoros of Myr went in alone, waving that flaming sword of his.
I suppose no one on the small council could speak sense to him.
In full plate metal for 16 hours. Never occurred to me till the fighting was over. I was very nearly the first man knighted to piss on the king's boots.
It suffered for a reason. I sold men into slavery.
It was a bitch of a siege.
King Robert wanted her dead.
No? Doesn't the Lord of the Kingsguard traditionally--
Which men do you have in mind?
With all my heart.
You only joined us a few days ago. I can't speak to your intentions.
You swore an oath.
You're not Lord here. You're just another exile. And I take my orders from the queen.
200,000 if not more.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: On the field, with ease. But they won't meet us on the field. They have provisions, patience, and strong walls. If they're wise, they'll hide behind those walls and chip away at us, man by man.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: We don't need Yunkai, khaleesi. Taking this city will not bring you any closer to Westeros or the Iron Throne.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I don't know.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Yunkai. The Yellow City.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: He's a dangerous man, khaleesi. They all are.
Only by the broken swords on their banners. They're called the Second Sons. A company led by a Braavosi named Mero, "The Titan's Bastard."
He may not agree to meet.
I imagine you're right.
Mind your tongue.
The Second Sons have faced worse odds and run.
They can be trusted to kill you if they're well paid. The Yunkish are paying them well.

Or perhaps you'll lead Grey Worm and me to the slaughter, cutting the head off our army.  The masters of Yunkai will pay you your fee and you won't have to split it three ways cause you've already slaughtered your partners.
You're the Queensguard, Ser Barristan.  Your place is by the Queen.  If we are truly her loyal servants, we'll do whatever needs to be done.  No matter the cost, no matter our pride.
[Addressing DAARIO] That’s what you call a few guards?
[Looks at DAENERYS]  It was just as you said.  They did not believe until it was too late.  Their slave soldiers threw down their spears and surrendered.
There may be others.
Very good.  We'll prepare.
You command the Unsullied.  What do you think?
Your men but not you?
You didn't conquer them. You liberated them.
They're dragons, Khaleesi. They can never be tamed. Not even by their mother.
There's one on every mile marker between here and Meereen.
I've been by your side longer than any of them, Khaleesi. Let me stand for you today as well.
A single rider. A champion of Meereen. They want you to send your own champion against him.
They're not meaningless if half the city you intend to take is listening to them.
163, Khaleesi.
It's hard to say. It could be enough. But we're not fighting to make you queen of King's Landing. 10,000 men can't conquer Westeros.
The old houses will flock to whichever side they think will win, as they always have.

There's other news. From Yunkai. Without the Unsullied to enforce your rule, the Wise Masters have retaken control of the city. They've reenslaved the freedmen who stayed behind and sworn to take revenge against you. And in Astapor, the council you installed to rule over the city has been overthrown by a butcher named Cleon who's declared himself "His Imperial Majesty."
What, then?
(CHUCKLES)
King Joffrey Baratheon is dead. Murdered at his own wedding.
The Lannisters have more.
You could sail for Westeros and leave it all behind. A boy sits on the Iron Throne. A boy many believe to be a bastard with no right to it. They've never been more vulnerable.
You're a Targaryen. You're the Mother of Dragons.

Herding the masters into pens and slaughtering them by the thousands is also treating men like beasts. The slaves you freed, brutality is all they've ever known. If you want them to know something else, you'll have to show it to them.
I sold men into slavery, Khaleesi.
It's not a question of approval, it's a question of trust.
It's tempting to see your enemies as evil, all of them, but there's good and evil on both sides in every war ever fought.
Without you there to rule, Khaleesi, I fear the masters will simply bide their time, wait for the invaders to leave and reassert control.
Yes, Khaleesi.
(Nods.)
Daario Naharis killed his captains and dumped their heads at your feet when he grew tired of their commands. How could you ever have faith in a man like that?
Hizdahr zo Loraq?
I did.
I wouldn't be here to help you if Ned Stark had done to me what you want to do to the masters of Yunkai.
Khaleesi.
Later than some.
Why would I? The man's a sellsword.
You have?
Daenerys, please.
Forgive me. I never meant...please, Khaleesi, forgive me.
I sent letters to Varys, the spymaster of King's Landing.
Who do you think sent this to Meereen? Who profits? This is the work of Tywin Lannister. He wants to divide us. If we're fighting each other, we're not fighting him.
Have I forgotten a council meeting?
Have you told her?
I have loved you.
I have protected you. Fought for you. Killed for you.
I stopped you from drinking his wine.
I suspected.
If we could speak alone.
Information.
It is not forged.
Khaleesi.
Let me speak with her in private.
Ser Barristan.
What's this?
When you and Viserys arrived in Pentos. His plan to marry you to Khal Drogo. When you were married. When your brother died.
Who gave you this?
Yes.
I'm taking you to the Queen.
I am. Queen Daenerys Targaryen. She's the Queen I serve.
I'm not taking you to your sister.
No.
Then stay awake.
What business would you have with the Queen?
Your captor.
"The city of a thousand years, and all that men had learned; The Doom consumed it all alike, and neither of them turned."
We walk, up the coast. With luck, we'll find a fishing village. Maybe another boat.
We've got a long walk ahead of us. I'll get some wood for a fire. Try to get some rest, huh?
(Faintly). Tyrion? Tyrion. Tyrion. Tyrion. You're all right.
And then they weren't.
Get behind!
I am.
I suppose that's why they send them there.
I think you'll be plenty.
No wine.
No.
No. But the pirates are.
Stone Men! Don't let them touch you!
We are.
You're heavier than you look. Did any of them touch you?
In something greater than ourselves, I mean. The Gods. Destiny. Do you believe there's a plan for this world?
It’s no lie. His name was Qotho. He was bloodrider to Khal Drogo. Take me to Slaver’s Bay, put a sword in my hand, I’ll prove my worth.

Neither did I. I was a cynic, just like you. Then I saw a girl step into a great fire with three stone eggs. When the fire burned out I thought I’d find her blackened bones. Instead I found her. Daenerys. Alive and unhurt. Holding her baby dragons. Have you ever heard baby dragons singing?
Do you believe in anything?
Do you ever shut up?
First we have to conquer the world.
How?
How?
I am not your travelling companion.
I can.
I killed a Dothraki blood rider in single combat.
I’m sure you’ve visited many brothels in many cities.
It's hard to be a cynic after that.
No it doesn’t.
Of course I’m hungry.
Queen Daenerys has outlawed slavery.
She is the rightful heir.
Shh! It’s a slave ship
Then she rules.
They probably came ashore for--
We better keep moving.
What do you know about my father?
Why?
Khaleesi, please, I just need a moment of your time. I brought you a gift.
Your Grace, I want to say--
I’ve been flogged if it makes you happy. I’m the best you’ve got. If I win at the Great Pit how much can you sell me for?
Let me fight for her and I belong to you.
You said whoever wins would fight at the Great Pit in front of the queen. I won.
I fight and die for your glory, oh glorious Queen.
This way!
Careful now.
He’s a foreign dwarf that barely speaks the language. Why would the Meereenese listen to him?
If I ever kill you your eyes will be wide open.
None of that matters now. The longer we sit here bantering the longer Daenerys is out there in the wilderness.
The second time thanks to you.
Torgo Nudho.
We? You’re a Lannister. The Queen intends to remove your family from power.
You’ve been here for how many days now? I’ve fought for her for years. Since she was little more than a child.
Don’t know anything else that can melt a ram’s horn.
I’ve been all over the world. There’s no escaping men like us.
If you grow old.
Not an army. A horde.
Of course it is.
Ram.
She’s too smart to like it.
So do I.
They have her.
You know why.

Eastern Market, Western Market. When Khal Drogo died, she was supposed to come here and join the Dosh Khaleen, the widows of the dead khals. That’s where they would have taken her, the Temple of the Dosh Khaleen.
If they find a body with a stab wound, the whole city will be looking for us.
If they spot us and we’re unarmed, we’ll say we’re traders heading for the Western Market. But if they see weapons…
The road running through the Horse Gate, they call that the godsway.
There’s 100,000 of them down there. We can’t fight our way out. We wait till dark, and then we’ll find her.
Wine. Come down to my stall tomorrow, I’ll give you a cask of the Arbor’s finest.
You didn’t get much discipline as a child, did you?
All we can do is try.
Come on.
Don’t worry. it didn’t touch you.
I know what happens.
I’m fine.
It’s forbidden to carry weapons in the sacred city.
Makes me sad. You’ll disappoint her before long. She’ll move on.
My friends...We wandered off from the Western Market and got lost. Could you show us the way back?
We have to go now.
We need each other right now. After we’re done needing each other --
What?
Don’t be. All I’ve ever wanted was to serve you. Tyrion Lannister was right. I love you. I’ll always love you. Goodbye, Khaleesi.
I don’t know that either. But I’ve seen what happens when it goes far enough. I’ll end things before that.
I don’t know.
You must send me away.
Has she come yet?
The Dragon Queen. Daenerys Stormborn.
But how long 'till...
Have you ever done this before?
How long?
Jorah.  Jorah Mormont.
There's no need.  I've been dead to them for years.
What are you doing?
What is it?
A bit.  Less than before.
And the climate.
How bad is it?
I don't know anything about that.  I just started feeling better.  I assumed it was the rest that did it.
I owe my life to her and you.
I surrendered to this sickness the moment I first saw it.  I knew it would kill me or I'd kill myself before it could.  Daenerys Stormborn convinced me otherwise.  The only place for me is with her.
Perhaps our paths will cross again?
I wouldn't be here if I hadn't.  I return to your service, my queen, if you'll have me.
You returned the favor, as I recall.
Your Grace, I --
Don't you want to know what we're doing?
He was my father.
I hardly recognized you.
It was supposed to last us the rest of our lives.
Thoros?
We were hoping some of your men could help.
With the queen's permission I'll go north and take one.
You asked me to find a cure so I could serve you.  Allow me to serve you.
Your Grace.
I can't think of a worse way for him to go.  The Night's Watch was his life.  He would have died to protect every one of those men, and they butchered him.
He gave it to you.
He was a good man.  He deserved a better son.  Were you with him at the end?
He was in the right of course.  It didn't make me hate him any less.
I brought shame to my house.  I broke my father's heart.
I forfeited the right to claim this sword.  It's yours.  May it serve you well and your children after you.
Me too.
Your father wanted to execute me.
Dragons don't understand the difference between what is theirs and what isn't. Land, livestock, children... Letting them roam free around a city was a problem.

Perhaps you should fly to Winterfell, Your Grace. You have many enemies in the North. Thousands fell fighting your fatherAll it takes is one angry man with a crossbow. He'll see your silver hair on the kingsroad and know that one well-placed bolt will make him a hero. The man who killed the conqueror.
They could, they just wouldn't.
But you did forgive, despite my failures. When I heard you'd named Tyrion your Hand, it broke my heart.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Forgive me, khaleesi.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I am. And one other suggestion, if you'll allow me.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Please, listen to me. You're the future of our house.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Thank you, my lady.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: As have we all. He owns his and learns from them.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: But the mind behind all those words-
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I didn't. His mouth hardly stopped moving between Volantis and Meereen. It was all I could do not to throw him in the sea.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I'll wield it in his memory to guard the realms of men.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Many things.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: We have all we need to win this war.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: What have you got there?
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You made the right choice.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You still have a family.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: You'll be safer in the crypt. These things we're fighting-
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: My lord! My lord! They found her. She's unharmed.
She's been taken directly before the King.
The Lannisters found her.
The Queen ordered them to bring her straight to him.
Yes, my Lord.
Forgive me, my Lord...
He said he'd be glad to talk to the Hand himself. He's a knight, you see.
I'm here on behalf of Lord Eddard Stark, the Hand of the king. I'm the captain of his guard.
My Lord, Her Grace the Queen.
No "ser." I'm not a knight.
We've met before, you know.
You shouldn't be out here, my Lord. There's no telling who has eyes where.
Aye. One of the Greyjoys nearly took my eye.
Can I leave this with you? The message from Lord Stark.
Find anything?
Ser Hugh? Ser Hugh!
The siege of Pyke. We fought side by side one afternoon.
Theon? He's a good lad.
They like their bloodshed.
This is for the King from Lord Stark.
Three? Four?
With his burning sword? I'll remember that till the day I die.
Come along, my Lady. You heard your father.
No fear on that count, little Lady.
Pardon, my Lord. There's a Night's Watchman here begging a word. He says it's urgent.
Right away, my Lord. Lord Baelish is here for you.
Stay back, Ser! This is the Hand of the King!
Here in King's Landing? Fifty.
Heward and Wyl.
If you threaten my Lord again...
My Lord.
Whose throat you gonna cut, old man?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You are a bastard. A daughter-fucking, wildling bastard.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: I'd rather eat what you've got hidden away.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: It's cold outside and there's nothing to eat.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: There are no laws beyond the Wall. Now show us where he hides the food or you'll get the same as he did.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: There's sawdust in the bread.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Any command for us, Lord Commander? What's that? Fuck 'em till they're dead?
Did you hear that, boys? Fuck 'em till they're dead.
Haven't lost a fight since I was nine. Maybe it's time. What do you think, eh?
I was a fucking legend in Gin Alley. A fucking legend! I would take any knight... any knight, any time. Fucking cunts in steel plate. Fucking cowards.
Maybe you're the man. Eh, cunt?
See you haven't played this game before. A highborn hostage, that's valuable. But three of them, that's a lot of mouths to feed.

You see, where I come from, a commoner like me slaps a little lord like you, I'd lose my right hand. But we're a long way from home, aren't we? And the two of you, fancy-looking folks north of the Wall creeping through the woods. Isn't that a bit odd?

You should shuty our fucking hole, ugly little cunt. You look likea fucking ballsack. Ugly. Look at your stupid cunt face. I could piss in any gutte rand soak five of you. Know how much they paid me to kill a man in King's Landing? Seven silvers. They told me a man's name and that man never saw daylight again. None of them cocksuckers got away from me.
And I thought this was gonnabe another boring day.
Come here.
Don't need another mouth to feed. Hand him over.
Go outside and feed the beast.
Help him up.
I like your curly hair. My mom had curls like that. Beautiful brown curls. Why'd you drag a crippled boy all the way up here?
Karl Tanner from Gin Alley drinking wine from the skull of Jeor fucking Mormont.
Rast is headed that way.
Rast.
Shut up!
So... if it worked for him... Let's give the walkers what they want.
This is nice. Fine leather. You're no wildling. Important. Highborn. Who are you?
To who?
What am I supposed to do with him? What did Craster do with 'em? Kill 'em before they could grow up and do the same to him? All right.
What the fuck is that?
Who are you?
Who are you?
Shh. Shh, shh, shh.
Some old man teach you how to stand, how to parry?

You've got pretty hair, don't you? Now what is a pretty little highborn girl like you doing out here in the deep, dark woods, eh? You left your daddy's castle looking for trouble, didn't you? No dresses for you. You like it rough, don't you? You like it in the gutter, don't you?
And how you gonna do that?
Don't close your eyes.
Get her up.
Have you seen what I'm gonna do to your sister? Have you seen what they're going to do to your sister?
How to fight with honor?
I've always liked a girl with curls. A touch of class. You take whatever's left.
Lord Snow. Are you bringing me back for trial?
Shh.
That's very helpful.
We had a good thing here. We were free men. You'll never be free. You'll never know what that's like.
What a fine thing. A fine thing.
You can help me?
You know what's wrong with honor?
You learn how to fight in a castle?
You.
Beat the white walkers? Good luck with that. Run from them, maybe.
I lost my father, my uncle, and 2 brothers fighting the damn crows.
I need to get the old folks on the boats. I’m right behind you, I promise. (she kisses them on the forehead) Go on.
Johnna is gonna look after you. She’s in charge, you listen to her.
So would mine, but fuck 'em, they’re dead.
You vouch for this man, Tormund?
And with the things we’ve seen you don’t believe them?
Come with you where?
Don’t think you’re gonna be there to enforce those orders !
I fucking hate Thenns.
If…?
So should you ! My little girl has gone on. They're gonna let them pass the wall even if you’re not there ?
There are old stories about Dragonglass.
You built that wall to keep us out.
At once, My Lord.
Jaime smashed the River Lords at the Golden Tooth, and now lays siege to Riverrun, Catelyn Stark's homeland.
Stark's son has called his banners.
The great hairy one insisted he must have two battleaxes. Heavy black steel, double-sided.
Our scouts tell us the Stark host has moved south from the Twins with Lord Frey's levies in tow. They're a day's march north.
They do seem rather ferocious.
Both Baratheon brothers have taken up against us. Jaime captured, his armies scattered. lt's a catastrophe. Perhaps we should sue for peace.
l'm told we still have his sisters.
Better than seeing their heads mounted on the city gates. Stannis will execute them all.
He sent a splinter force to recapture Winterfell. The Greyjoys have done us a great favor. Stark won't risk marching on Casterly Rock until he's at full force.
King's Landing will fall an hour after Stannis lands his force. It's not too late for King Joffrey and Cersei and the court to ride west to safety.
The scouts assure us Robb Stark remains north of Ashemark.
They call themselves sparrows. Bloody fanatics. Religion has its place, of course, but at a certain point… They never would have come to the capital when Tywin was alive.
I apologize for my son’s appearance.
Of course.
He should be here, learning what it means to rule.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Yield! I yield.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Your vassals are starting to snigger behind your back. Brides aren't usually virgins two weeks after their wedding night.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: He would have been a true king, a good king.
Ride back to Highgarden, sister. I'm not running from Stannis.
And you want that privilege for yourself.
Get out.
I don't believe that. You don't believe that. Who gained the most from our king's death?
I will put a sword through his righteous face.
Revenge.
Your Grace, my sister Margaery, her husband was taken from us before . . . She remains innocent. I would ask you to find it in your heart to do us the great honor of joining our houses.
Margaery does a great deal of work with the poor back in Highgarden.
Yes.
Your Grace.
I believe you grow more beautiful every day, Lady Sansa.
I'll take my leave. Lady Sansa.
Lady Sansa.
My sister Margaery invites you to take the air with her and our grandmother the Lady Olenna in the gardens. May I escort you to them?
Of course I did.
You have a good deal of experience, do you?
(chuckles)
How did you know?
My lord I'll have you know I'm to be married soon and even my intended hasn't the slightest notion.
Squire!
That I wanted to.
That was no fight. I don't know you.
It's terrible, isn't it? The most terrible place there is.
Oh, it's more of a brooch, really. Though I suppose a brooch is a sort of pin, so
Uh, yes. I-I am as well.

Yes, me, too. Definitely. I've dreamed of a large wedding since I was quite young. The guests, the food, the tournaments. And the bride, of course. The most beautiful bride in the world in a beautiful gown of gold and green brocade with fringed sleeves. Have you ever been to Highgarden, my lady?
Well, my father once told me--
Ser Jaime, I'm very sorry.
Yes, very much.
And neither will you.
As does yours.
They certainly are.
If she doesn’t marry me, she doesn’t go to Highgarden. Which means she stays in King’s Landing. Which means you’re trapped here with Cersei Lannister as your mother-by-law.
It was such a deep, deep shock to us all. Your father was a force to be reckoned with. He truly was. I wouldn’t presume to claim to have known him as such…
Judging by my experience, that would be wonderful. Dorne, Highgarden. Anywhere but here.
But just being in his presence was enough to make it so clear just how formidable a person you were dealing with. What a… what a force to be reckoned with.
Fine.
It’s Dorne.
My intended? Please. Tywin’s dead. That means no one can force Cersei to marry me.
No it doesn’t.
Perhaps?
Sandstone.
Unlucky you.
Why? They all know about me anyway. Everybody knows everything about everyone. What’s the point in trying to keep a secret in a place like this?
You’re very respectful.
Get your hands off of me.
Who do you think you are?
He was my friend. He was my king.
I don’t pay attention to rumors.
I was wrong to support Renly’s claim. I know that. But I was forgiven by Joffrey. I fought for him at the Battle of the Blackwater.
Liar!
Liar! He’s a liar!
Never.
Never.
Of course I deny them.
Why does it matter what I wore?
I can’t stay strong. I never was strong.
I don’t care about that.
I just want it to stop. Help me.
Let them win. Just make it stop. Please.
I perjured myself before the gods. I am guilty of depravity… dishonesty, profligacy, and arrogance. I see that now. I humble myself before the Seven and accept whatever punishment the gods deem just.
All of them. I lay with other men including the traitor Renly Baratheon.
I do. I will abandon the Tyrell name and all that goes with it. I will renounce my lordship and my claims on Highgarden. I will never marry and I will never father children.

I take full responsibility for my many sins and unburden myself of my desires. My only remaining wish is to devote my life to the Seven. May I be a living example of their grace for others to witness.
I will.
There’ll be no need for a trial. I confess before the Seven and freely admit to my crimes.
He runs, I'll chop his balls off.
He's not yours to kill, crow.
And now he wishes he did. Gut him.
I already got one crow. Don't need two.
The Halfhand knows more. This one is just a little boy. Gut him.
What does Mance want with a dead man's bastard?
Burn the body.
Enough of that!
Let 'em fight.
You don't want this one coming back for you.
We killed his friends. Thought you'd want to question this one.
Is that right? You and the pretty crow do a lot of talking, Tormund. And when you’re done talking, do you get down on your knees and suck his cock--
Last time I saw you the little crow was your prisoner. The other way around now. What happened?
What is he?
Why aren’t you in chains?
You call that a war? The greatest army the North has ever seen cut to pieces by a southern king.
You don’t give the orders here.
You fucking traitor! You fight for the crows now.
Father is old. It will put his heart at peace if he could see her wed to a good husband.  Black WALDER: And his recent experience has made him wary of long engagements.

Our father has instructed us to tell you that his alliance with the North can continue if his terms are met. Lord Frey requires a formal apology for your violation of your sacred oath to marry one of his daughters.
They ought to be because they cost enough.
He’ll never yield, Father.
The Brotherhood Without Banners is rallying the commoners against us, raiding our supply trains and camps.
He surprised us. He knows the castle better than anyone.
Apologies, Ser Jaime.
Come out and fight us, Blackfish. Yield the castle or we’ll hang him.
Do as he says.
Ser Jaime. We didn’t know you were coming.
This is your last warning. Yield the castle.
Walder.
All right, Ser Davos of House Seaworth. How is it you understand how I feel?
As far as I understand, you’re a Snow and Lady Sansa is a Bolton. Or is she a Lannister? I’ve heard conflicting reports.
Go on, Ser Davos.
House Mormont has kept faith with House Stark for 1,000 years. We will not break faith today.
I don’t know you, Ser…?
I doubt it. My mother wasn’t a great beauty or any other kind of beauty. She was a great warrior, though. She died fighting for your brother, Robb.
If you say so. In any case, you don’t just want my allegiance. You want my fighting men.
We are not a large house, but we’re a proud one. And every man from Bear Island fights with the strength of 10 mainlanders.
I remember what it said. “Bear Island knows no king but the King in the North whose name is Stark.”
I think we’ve had enough small talk. What are you here?
I understand that I’m responsible for Bear Island and all who live here. So why should I sacrifice one more Mormont life for someone else’s war?
Is this true?
Welcome to Bear Island.
I’ve missed you, big brother.
If Robert finds out, he’ll kill him, you know he will. You have to protect him. Promise me, Ned. Promise me.
No, no water.
Please. Listen to me, Ned.
Promise me, Ned. Promise me.

Your son was butchered at the Red Wedding, Lord Manderly. But you refused the call. You swore allegiance to House Stark, Lord Glover, but in their hour of greatest need, you refused the call. And you, Lord Cerwyn, your father was skinned alive by Ramsay Bolton. Still you refuse the call. But House Mormont remembers. The North remembers. We know no king but the King in the North whose name is Stark. I don’t care if he’s a bastard. Ned Stark’s blood runs through his veins. He’s my king from this day until his last day.
I don’t want to die.
I want to be brave.
I’m not.
Is that you? Is that really you?
Ned?
You’re not a dream.
I don't plan on knitting by the fire while men fight for me. I might be small, Lord Glover, and I might be a girl, but I am every bit as much a Northerner as you.
We'll begin training every man, woman, boy, and girl on Bear Island.
And I don't need your permission to defend the North.
Winter is here, Your Grace.  We need the King in the North in the north.
Father, Smith, Warrior, Mother, Maiden, Crone, Stranger. I am his and he is mine from this day until the end of my days.
His name is Aegon.
"Your Grace."
A lord? Nothing at all?
Not important? We named you King in the North.
I have trained my men, women and children. I have fought before. I can fight again.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I will not hide underground. I pledged to fight for the North, and I will fight.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I wish you good fortune, cousin.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: I don't need you to remind me of that.
EpisodeName: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

Text: Benjen always lifts his chin when he’s about to charge.
What about him?
Who are you gonna spar with when Ned goes off to the Eyrie?
Perhaps you will, my little love.
Ser Vardis, my sister's guest is weary. Take him down below so he can rest. Introduce him to Mord.
Shh... Shh... Shh, my sweet boy. He's just trying to frighten us. Lannisters are all liars. No one will hurt my baby.

You bring him here without permission? You pollute my home with his presence? Your aunt has done a bad thing, Robin, a very bad thing. You remember her, don't you? Isn't he beautiful? And strong too. Jon knew it. His last words were, "the seed is strong." He wanted everyone to know what a good, strong boy his son would grow up to be. Look at him, the Lord of all the Vale.
He's Tyrion the Imp of House Lannister. He killed your father. He murdered the Hand of the King!
It is.
To stay away from them! Not to bring one here!
You will watch your tongue! These men are Knights of the Vale. Every one of the loved Jon Arryn. Every one of them would die for me.
If you’re tried and found guilty, then by the king’s own laws you will pay with your life.
Skycells always break them. Speak, Imp. Meet your gods as an honest man.
You want a trial, my Lord Lannister. Very well. My son will listen to whatever you have to say, and you will hear his judgement. Then you will leave, by one door or the other.
You’ve had your little joke. I trust you enjoyed it. Mord, take him back to the dungeon. But this time find a smaller cell, with a steeper floor.
I think that we can assume that no one is willing -
Ser Vardis… You’re quiet. Did you want to avenge my husband?
Silence!
The Kingslayer is hundreds of miles from here.
The trial will be today.
We have no executioner in the Eyrie. Life is more elegant here. Open the Moon Door.
What do you think you’re doing?
You demanded a trial by combat.
You don’t fight with honor!
You have that right.
You wish to confess your crimes?
Go on, sweet. Time for your bath. I'll feed you after.
He sent it to me, not you.
Hush now, darling. You just ate.
Soon, love, soon.
The knights of the Vale will stay in the Vale where they belong, to protect their Lord.
A war? Your son against the Lannisters?
Family means everything to me.
Of course I fear for the safety of my son!

Oh, yes. At suppertime, she would go straight for honey cakes, candied almonds, custard. Anything sweet. Eventually, your grandfather had to assign a septa to watch her at meals. Cat was the firstborn daughter after all. It was important that she remain desirable so Father could arrange a good match for her and she was starting to get fat.
And on top of everything else, they made you marry that filthy troll.
Good. Robin, this is your cousin Sansa. But you're not to call her Sansa in front of anyone but Uncle Petyr and myself. Do you understand? Sansa, this is my son Robin.
I'm done waiting, Petyr. We had our wedding night many years ago. Or don't you remember?
I'm warning you. I'm going to scream when my husband makes love to me. I'm going to scream so loud, they'll hear me clear across the Narrow Sea.

Oh, do take down that hood, child. Don't you think know who you are? You think I'd let my intended leave the Eyrie on urgent business without knowing what that business was? I let him go so he could bring you here to me.

Oh, gods, no. Petyr had three crates brought all the way from King's Landing. He knew you liked lemon cakes. He's so kind. He really cares for you. Think where you'd be without him. In their clutches and tried for murder.
Robin, show Sansa to her chamber. Take the back stairs.
There's only one Lord of the Vale. The others can all hang. Lurking and simpering on the stairs like buzzards the moment my husband died, trying to get their claws in me.

This is before she married your father and moved to the North. By the time you were born, your father's austerity had become hers. Marriage changes people. I didn't mean for you to stop. Go on, enjoy them. How do you like them?

What wife would do for you the things I've done for you? What wife would trust you the way I've trusted you? When you gave me those drops and told me to pour them into Jon's wine, my husband's wine... when you told me to write a letter to Cat telling her it was the Lannisters...
Your mother always had a sweet tooth, you know?

Your mother never loved him. Never. Cat always went straight for the sweetest thing. The most obvious thing. Your Uncle Brandon. Your handsome, arrogant, cruel Uncle Brandon. He almost killed Petyr in a duel. And your mother loved him anyway. And now Petyr is risking his life to save you, the daughter of a woman who didn't love him no more than those whores in his brothels. Has he told you about them?
Your young, pretty body.
(LAUGHS)
(nods)
A beautiful gift for a beautiful boy.
Are you pregnant?
Don't lie to me.
He feels responsible for you.
He hasn't told you about the vile things they do with their bodies? The vile things they let him do with their bodies?
I don't believe that for a moment. Did he force himself on you?
I wasn't asking about Tyrion. What have you let Petyr do with your body?
I'll know if you lie.
Let's get married tonight.
Lord Baelish.
Loved your mother.
My flesh and blood.
No one can know you're here. It would put us in a very precarious position.
She's here. We've spent more than enough time on her for one evening.

Shh. Shh. Shh. It's all right. It will all be all right. You'll be a widow soon. They'll execute that dwarf for murdering the king and you'll be free to marry Robin. You'll be the Lady of the Vale. Hmm.
That's what you wanted to say.
The Lannisters want to destroy us. They've been trying for years. Now they know what it feels like.
What took you so long?
Why? Why does he feel responsible for you?
You mustn't call me that in front of anyone else.
Come here, Sansa.
Don't be coy with me, you little whore. You kissed him. You kissed Petyr.
My father, my husband, my sister, they all stood between us and now they're all dead. That's what happens to people who stand between Petyr and me. Look down! Look down! Look down! Look down!

Neither do I, precisely. Hundreds of feet. It's fascinating what happens to bodies when they hit the rocks from such a height. The impact breaks them right apart. Like eggs dropped on the floor. Sometimes pieces remain intact. You'll find the head sitting on its own. Every hair in place. Blue eyes staring at nothing. I know what you did.
Do you know how far the fall is?
I saw you! You can't lie to me because I saw it with my own eyes.
Liar! Whore! He is mine!
She's just like her mother. She'll never love you. I lied for you. I killed for you. Why did you bring her here? Why?
You want her? This empty-headed child?
From House Tyrell and the people of the Reach, Your Grace, it is my honor to present you with this wedding cup.
I shall be honored, Your Grace.
May you and my daughter Margaery drink deep and live long.
Lord Tywin, it's a great honor to have been granted a seat on this council. I--
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: And he said this to you at a meeting of the small council?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Do you remember the precise nature of this threat?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: I beg your pardon? You said you were his...
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Lord Tywin and I already determined that I shall be the Master of Ships long before you--
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Your own brother said this to you?
EpisodeName: The Laws of Gods and Men

Text: Oh, Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: The honor is all mine, Your Grace.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Your Grace, I would be willing to serve provisionally as the King's Hand until he becomes old enough--
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: High Septon, this is a rather shocking thing to hear.
(After a moment, looks at her surprised). Me? (Qyburn smiles).
(Raises his hands). Of course, of course, (Gathers his belongings, and then gets up). I'll give your regards to the Titan of Braavos.
I will be honored, Your Grace.
Well, House Tyrell could front the gold, and the the Crown will pay us back in time, or I'll have words with my daughter.
With winter coming, half what they ask. Less.
Absolutely.
My very own Kingsguard? Please express -
The Iron Bank has called one tenth of the Crown's debts. Given the expense of rebuilding the royal -
If a man charges low interest on a loan then he has nothing to gain and everything to lose, so why charge him? Whereas a reward makes a man willing to gamble.

It's not a gift, it’s a skill, anyone can learn it. “So give me a kiss by the Long Canall and give me two kisses in Salty Town, for we are going to die, tomorrow. The bareheaded beggar, the king with his crown…”
Oh, nonsense. Work's over. Do you sing?
Some consider usury distasteful, dishonorable. Pure nonsense, of course.
Vintnors say this might be the best year for red grapes in half a century. If we come to a satisfactory arrangement, I’ll be sure to send the Arbor’s finest your way.
Bit of chop the last few days but nothing too awful. Good to see the old chap is still guarding the Harbor.
Did you know that at one point Maegor the third tried to outlaw it in the Seven Kingdoms? Wanted to arrest anyone caught charging interest and cut off both their hands.
You are the world's best gamblers. And all those bets you won built this.
My mother...
My friends, the hour has come. Madness has overtaken this city and grasped in its claws my children. But now we must drive it back under the rocks whence it came. Madness has had its day!
My lord.
What’s happening?
No, I can’t let them…
I have just the one. Anara our most expensive girl, but well worth it, I assure you.
I’ll have you whipped, girl! Go on!
Brea.
Of course.
Of course. Of course.
Who let this girl in? Shoo shoo!
Prince Doran.
According to our records, Jeor Mormont is Lord Commander.
The archmaester will discuss these irregularities with you. In the meantime, you are permitted to use the library. Follow me.
No women or children!
Oh.
This is irregular.
We received no report from the maester at Castle Black.
It brings to mind the work of Davy Moonstone, the charlatan who claimed to see the children of the forest.
Horrible business.
How many winters have you seen, Lord Tyrion?
When winter does come, gods help us all if we're not ready.
All of them brief?

The Night's Watch is the only thing standing between the realm and what lies beyond. And it has become an army of undisciplined boys and tired old men. There are less than a thousand of us now. We can't man the other castles on the Wall. We can't properly patrol the wilderness. We've barely enough resources to keep our lads armed and fed.

This summer has lasted nine. But reports from the Citadel tell us the days grow shorter. The Starks are always right eventually : winter is coming. This one will be long and dark things will come with it.
Samwell, you will assist me in the rookery and library.
As you wish.
Certainly. And keep a fire burning in his chambers.
We took you for a man of The Night's Watch.
l did indeed. Perhaps you would be kind enough to assist me. Tell me, did you ever wonder why the men of the Night's Watch take no wives and father no children?

l'm a maester of the Citadel, bound in service to Castle Black and the Night's Watch. l will not tell you to stay or go. You must make that choice yourself and live with it for the rest of your days. As l have.
My father was Maekar, the first of his name. My brother Aegon reigned after him when l had refused the throne. And he was followed by his son Aerys whom they called the Mad King.
Oh, it hurts, boy. Oh, yes. l know.

So they will not love. Love is the death of duty. lf the day should ever come when your lord father was forced to choose between honor on the one hand and those he loves on the other, what would he do?

The gods were cruel when they saw fit to test my vows. They waited till l was old. What could l do when the ravens brought the news from the South: the ruin of my House, the death of my family? l was helpless, blind, frail. But when l heard they had killed my brother's son and his poor son and the children! Even the little children.
Then Lord Stark is one man in 10,000. Most of us are not so strong. What is honor compared to a woman's love? And what is duty against the feel of a newborn son in your arms? Or a brother's smile?
We're all human. Oh, we all do our duty when there's no cost to it. Honor comes easy then. Yet sooner or later in every man's life there comes a day when it is not easy. A day when he must choose.
Ah, for the gillyflower. Lovely.
Gilly, you and your son will be our guests for the time being. We certainly cannot send you back beyond the Wall.
Good. Samwell, fetch a quill and inkwell. I hope your penmanship is better than your swordplay.
I'm not a lord, my dear. Not for many, many years. Every man who joins the Night's Watch renounces all former titles. Among other things. What is your name?
And the child?
Tarly do you remember the oath you swore when you joined this order?
We had 44 ravens at last count. Make sure they're all fed. Every one of them flies tonight.
If we beheaded every ranger who lay with a girl, the Wall would be manned by headless men.
None of us are free. We are men of the Night's Watch. But we won't be taking your head today, Jon Snow. Go on.
Yes, you are.
He told the truth.
I grew up in King's Landing.
We have just over 100 men. And that's including stewards and builders. And me. We can't afford to lose a single man. We must remember our first responsibility. We are the watchers on the Wall.
Ah, the Wildlings. I can assure you that the closest Maester Faull ever got to a Wildling was this very library.
And what is it that couldn't wait until morning, Tarly?

Oh, no need. I know my way around this library better than any other place in Castle Black. Thousands of books and no eyes to read them. Old age is a wonderful source of ironies if nothing else. But you didn't answer my question, what are you reading?
You can imagine all manner of horrors befalling that poor girl and her child, is it so difficult to imagine that an old person was once, more or less, like you?

Ah ha. I could tell you everything about her. Who she was. How we met. The color of her eyes and the shape of her nose. I can see her right in front of me. She's more real than you are. We could spend all night trading tales of lost love. Nothing makes the past a sweeter place to visit than the prospect of imminent death. Go to bed Tarly.
That it was you? Who else but Samwell Tarly would be wasting candles to read in the middle of the night?
Yes you do! I heard it in your voice when you first brought her to me. I remember how it sounds! I was in love once.
Yes you do.

Yes. Imagine the stories Wildlings tell about us! Love is the death of duty. I told that to your friend Jon Snow once. He didn't listen and neither did you. That's why you abandoned your watch atop the wall to come here and read about the things that may have happened to the girl you love.

You know who I was before I came here? What I could've been if I'd only said the word? Of course you do. I met many girls when I was Aemon Targaryen. A future king always does. Some of them were quite forward in their attempts to win my affections. One of them succeeded. I was very young.

They came to us from White Harbor and Barrowton, from Fairmarket and King's Landing, from north and south, from east and west. They died protecting men, women, and children who will never know their names. It is for us to remember them. Our brothers, we shall never see their like again.
And now their watch is ended.
If there's no one else, we will begin the voting. The triangular tokens count for Ser Alliser Thorne. The square tokens for Ser Denys Mallister. Each brother will--
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Does anyone wish to speak for candidates before we cast our tokens for the 998th Lord Commander of the Night's Watch?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Is there anyone else?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: It is time.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Samwell Tarly. Go on.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: A Targaryen, alone in the world, is a terrible thing.
And she's alone, under siege, no family to guide her or protect her. Her last relation thousands of miles away, useless, dying.
Half the men hate you already, Lord Commander. Do it.
Oh, like a hundred-year-old man slowly freezing to death.

That doesn't matter. You do. You will find little joy in your command. But with luck, you will find the strength to do what needs to be done. Kill the boy, Jon Snow. Winter is almost upon us. Kill the boy, and let the man be born.
Lord Commander.
And before that, he was a jolly fellow. Like this one.
Get him south, Gillyflower. Before it’s too late.
Egg! Egg! Mothers looking for you! Egg!
Egg! Egg.. I dreamed... that I was old
There he is. That laugh. Egg! Egg laughed like that. One of the first things I remember about him.
(Scoffs) King. Since that boar killed his brother, every lord wants a coronation.
(Stands up). I owe you an apology, my King. My duty is to serve. You have chosen the new God over the Old Gods. May the Lord of Light watch over us all. Shall we drink together?
(Walks forward, interrupting Melisandre). All you men were named in the Light of the Seven! Is this how you treat the Gods of your fathers? Are you so eager to spit on your ancestors?
As do I, but loyal service means telling hard truths. He's surrounded by fools and fanatics, but he trusts you, Davos. (Davos picks up the sword and inspects it).  If you tell him the truth…
(To Davos) We need to stop her!
A cup of wine to honor the one true God.
This woman will lead him into a war he cannot win.
If this news is true, and the Lannisters conspire against the throne, who but you can protect the king?
It’s Maester Luwin, my lord.
Pardon, my lord, my lady. A rider in the night from your sister.
The king rode for a month to ask Lord Stark’s help. He’s the only one he trusts. You swore the king an oath, my lord.
A different time. Different king.
I’m told he drinks all night.
We’ve brought up eight barrels of ale from the cellar. Perhaps we’ll find out.
It's time we reviewed the accounts, my lady. You'll want to know how much this royal visit has cost us.
Poole went south with Lord Stark, my lady. We need a new steward, and there are several other appointments that require our immediate attention...
Very good, my Lord. My Lady.
What, is there going to be a battle in the Godswood? Too easily words of war become acts of war. We don't know the truth yet. Lord Stark must be told of this.
The boy was always sure-footed before.
He has no memory of that day.
The boy has lost the use of his legs.
Hmm... If the saddle Lord Tyrion designed actually works, you could learn to shoot a bow from horseback.
I still can't tell you, but she will be home soon.
No. A common saying, but not their official motto.
When you were born, I was the one who pulled you from your mother. I placed you in her arms. From that moment until the moment she dies, she will love you. Absolutely. Fiercely.
: Mhmm.
And failed rebellions.
And where is that written?
Bran...
Dothraki boys learn when they're four years old. Why shouldn't you?
Good.
Hmm...
House Hornwood.
Lords?
No, I don't.
Sometimes I worry you're too smart for your own good.
That's House Martell.
Those are Tully words... Your mother's. Are we playing a game?
We're still on their words.
You do know them. Think.
You know it is.
Your mother had to leave Winterfell to protect the family.
Your mother sat by your bed for three weeks while you slept.
Are the two mutually exclusive in your experience?
Chances are I won't be nearby the next time that happens.
Owls and Shadowcats and...
Theon Greyjoy! The lady is our guest.
Why did you come here?
Why? What are you afraid of?
It is your sister's hand, but the Queen's words.
All of them, My Lord?
This is a royal command, My Lord.
They have.
Yeah.
Bran…
We can spare four masons for a week, My Lord. Will that be sufficient to repair your walls?
We didn't want him here all day, did we?
Maintenance of a Holdfast generally falls to the lord of that Holdfast.
Nor did I. But listening to people you'd rather not listen to is one of your responsibilities as Lord of Winterfell. (Bran sighs). Lord Portan.
