EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: Which is Sansa Stark?
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: You must despise him. You're working so hard to undermine him.
EpisodeName: And Now His Watch Is Ended

Text: As is a royal wedding. The people are hungry for more than just food. They crave distractions. And if we don't provide them, they'll create their own. And their distractions are likely to end with us being torn to pieces. A royal wedding is much safer. Wouldn't you say?
Gods, boy, that's enough. We're not in a tavern.

I can't think how it slipped my mind. What is it, 12,000 infantrymen the Tyrell family has supplied? 2,000 in support. Provisions so this city might survive the winter. A million bushels of wheat. Half a million bushels each of barley, oats, and rye. 20,000 head of cattle. 50,000 sheep. You don't have to lecture me about wartime expenses. I'm quite familiar with them.
I was told you were drunk, impertinent, and thoroughly debauched. You can imagine my disappointment at finding nothing but a browbeaten bookkeeper. Where did you go for them, Volantis?
Oh, I'd nearly forgotten.
Oh, very well. I won't have it said that House Tyrell refuses to play its part. We'll pay for half the expenses and the celebrations will go on as planned. Is that sufficient?
Very good, then. That's settled. Good day.
And traditionally paid for by the royal family.
And?
Good.
I climbed all those steps to discuss financial matters?
Naturally.
No need to speak. Are there any figs? Fetch some. I always take figs midafternoon. They help move the bowels. To what do I owe this summons?
What good is the word extravagant if it can't be used to describe a royal wedding?
And if Cersei is too old to give Loras children, we're throwing another prized flower into the dirt. It is a chance we simply cannot take.
As an authority on myself, I must disagree.
Did you grow up with boy cousins, Lord Tywin? Sons of your father's bannermen, squires, stable boys?
I congratulate you upon your restraint. But it's a natural thing, two boys having a go at each other beneath the sheets.
Lie or not, you must admit many people find it quite convincing. Convincing enough to put swords in their hands and send them off to kill Lannisters and Tyrells thanks to our new affiliation.
Oh, not at all. A sword swallower through and through.

Old. I'm something of an expert on the subject. Her change will be upon her before long. I'll spare you the details of what will happen then. You men may have a stomach for bloodshed and slaughter, but this is another matter entirely.
And you never--
Impossible.
My grandson is the pride of Highgarden. The most desirable bachelor in all seven kingdoms. Your daughter--
Not once? Not in any way?
Oh
Old.
You would have your grandson protected by someone who disgusts you?

So their son will be your nephew. After you're wed to Cersei, of course. And you will be the king's stepfather and brother-in-law. When you marry the king, Joffrey's mother will become his sister-in-law and your son will be Loras' nephew?  Grandson?  I'm not sure. But your brother will become your father-in-law. That much is beyond dispute.
My little dears. Go and speak to the jewelers of King's Landing. Tell them who you are, tell them who sent you. The one who brings me the best necklace will get to keep the next best.
No. No. You're a queen, not an ox.
The Margaery Tyrell who walks into the sept a fortnight from now will inspire 1,000 songs. Now, how sad it will be if she's wearing rubbish like that.
You watch that. Even here, even with me.
I hear you knocked my grandson into the dirt like the silly little boy he is.
My word.
Nonsense.
We know who you are. We've heard all about you. But hearing is one thing. Aren't you just marvelous? Absolutely singular.
You dare not refuse.
Your grandfather gave me a necklace just like this one for my 51st nameday.
Anyway, I don't know what you're complaining about. I'm paying my share.
Idiots, help your king. -

Lord Tyrion, you see? Not as bad as all that. Perhaps if your pauper husband were to sell his mule and his last pair of shoes, he might be able to afford to bring you to Highgarden for a visit. Now that peace has come and all is right with the world, it would do you good to see some of it. You must excuse me. It's time I ate some of this food I paid for.

No, your heartfelt thank-you is it's own reward. I would imagine I'd be hearing it again before long. Wars are rather expensive. "The Iron Bank will have it's due." How they love to remind everyone. Almost as much as you Lannisters with your debts.
Not now, Mace. Lord Tywin and I are speaking.
We both know you're smarter than that. Come, Tywin, let us celebrate young love.

You look exquisite, child. The wind has been at you, though. I haven't had the opportunity to tell you how sorry I was to hear about your brother. War is war, but killing a man at a wedding, horrid. What sort of monster would do such a thing? As if men need more reasons to fear marriage.
Help the poor boy.
It feels proportionate.
The expected extravagance.
You ought to try enjoying something before you die. You might find it suits you.

More than you were with Renly. Less than you would have been if Joffrey had done you the courtesy of consummating the marriage before dying. In any case, this would not be an opportune moment to press the issue.
Nonsense. Your circumstances have improved markedly. You may not have enjoyed watching him die, but you enjoyed it more than you would have enjoyed being married to him, I can promise you that.
The world is overflowing with horrible things. But they're all a tray of cakes next to death. They brought me your grandfather's body when he died, you know? Made me look at it.
Our alliance with the Lannisters remains every bit as necessary to them as it is unpleasant for us. You did wonderful work on Joffrey. The next one should be easier.

They took me to the Great Hall and there he was. The man I'd married and suffered to father my children. A great doughy lump I'd sat next to at endless dinners and tedious gatherings. There he was... lying on a table.

And once he could, the only thing he wanted was what I'd given him the night before. I was good. I was very, very good. You are even better. But you need to act quickly. Cersei may be vicious, but she's not stupid. She'll turn the boy against you as soon as she can. And by the time you're married, it'll be too late. Luckily for you ,the Queen Regent is rather distracted at the moment, mourning her dear departed boy. Accusing her brother of his murder, which he didn't commit.
But I do know. You don't think I'd let you marry that beast, do you?
He could have done,but he didn't.

I wasn't originally meant to marry your grandfather Luthor, you know. He was engaged to my sister, your great-aunt Viola. I was to be given to some Targaryen or other. Marrying a Targaryen was all the rage back then. But the momentI saw my intended, with his twitchy little ferret's face and ludicrous silver hair, I knew he wouldn't do. So the evening before Luthor was to propose to my sister, I got lost on my way back from my embroidery lesson and happened upon his chamber.
The following morning, Luthor never made it down the stairs to propose to my sister 'cause the boy couldn't bloody walk.

The time has come, my dear. There's nothing more tedious than a trial. Except perhaps these gardens. If I have to take one more leisurely stroll through these gardens, I'll fling myself from the cliffs.
Have you been to see Tommen yet?
How absentminded of me.
Shh. Don't you worry yourself about all that. You just do what needs to be done.
And Loras is the queen’s brother. No, no, no, no, this is unacceptable.
Has the crown suddenly stopped needing the troops, gold, and wheat my house supplies?
Of course she is. She wants to drag our names through the dirt. Put us in our place. Get some rest, dear, you look appalling. Let me deal with Cersei Lannister.
Oh! You can smell the shit from 5 miles away. Well, why are we stopped, go on.
Put the pen down, dear, we all know you aren’t writing anything.
Rumors and gossip, that’s all they got. It’s theater, nothing more. If they arrested all the pillow biters in King’s Landing there’d be no room left in the dungeons for anyone else.
Your Grace, I have travelled a long way.
And the famous tart Queen Cersei.
Do you expect the alliance to continue after you’ve thrown our future into prison?
I didn’t trust your father. I didn’t particularly like him. But I respected him. He was no fool. He understood that sometimes we must work with our rivals rather than destroy them.
I know you’re behind this absurd incarceration of my grandson.
Renly Baratheon shagged half the stable boys in the Seven Kingdoms. Everyone knew. No one cared.
Well I think that’s quite enough of that.
What are you doing? Let us pass.
What veil?
You’ll get your apology when I get Loras.
A man of the people, is that your game? It’s an old game. dull and unconvincing. A man of the people who does Cersei’s dirty work for her.
Don’t pretend to have any concern for my house, my grandchildren, or me. I should have known you’d return to the capital once things started going wrong.

Half the men, women, and children in this foul city break the sacred laws. You live among murderers, thieves, and rapists. And yet you punish Loras for shagging and Margaery for defending her brother?

I promise you, Lord Baelish, that our fates are joined. Together we murdered a king. And if my house should fall, I would have nothing to hide. And if i should meet with some accident here at this broken little flesh market, they’ll never even find what’s left of you. So do you deny a part in all of this?
If it’s equality you want then so be it. When House Tyrell stops sending our crops to the capital, everyone here will starve. And I’ll make sure the hungry know who to blame,
Not for your clientele, clearly.
Spare me the homilies, I can smell a fraud from a mile away.
You there! Where would I find the High Septon, or High Sparrow, or whatever bloody fool name he’s got.
You’ve always been rather impressed with yourself, haven’t you?
And what did she want?
Don’t spar with me little fellow.
Don’t you walk away from me.
Hips.
How do they communicate their demands? By raven or horse?
I’m here for my grandson and granddaughter.
I’ve read the seven pointed star.
No you’re not.
What is it you want? Gold? I’ll make you the richest septon who ever lived.
What kind of gift?
What then?
You should have the decency to stand when you talk to a lady.
I was invited, my dear, o help deal with several troublesome issues, such as the queen’s imprisonment.
Margaery is the queen. You are not the queen because you’re not married to the king. I do appreciate these things can get a bit confusing in your family.
My dear, you have been stripped of your dignity and authority, publicly shamed, and confined to the Red Keep. What’s left to work with?
Oh, no. That cannot happen. That will not happen.
I would have thought we were perfectly clear the first time. You’re not welcome.
Many will die no matter what we do. Better them than us.
He’s beaten us. That’s what’s happening.
He is the heir to Highgarden, the future of House Tyrell.

I wonder if you’re the worst person I’ve ever met. At a certain age, it’s hard to recall. But the truly vile do stand out through the years. Do you remember the way you smirked at me when my grandson and granddaughter were dragged off to their cells? I do. I’ll never forget it.
I’ll see you soon, my dear.
I’m leaving this wretched city as fast as I can before that shoeless zealot throws me into one of his cells. If you’re half as bright as you think you are, you’ll find your way out of here, too.
Oh, this is madness!

What’ll you do, then? You have no support. Not anymore. Your brother’s gone. The High Sparrow saw to that. The rest of your family have abandoned you. The people despise you. You’re surrounded by enemies, thousands of them. You’re going to kill them all by yourself? You’ve lost, Cersei. It’s the only joy I can find in all this misery.
You’re not in your sanctuary now, my dear. All I have to do is whistle and my men will stroll in here and bash you about...
...until I tell them to stop. If I tell them to stop. You could use a good bashing.
And what about your brother? What mercy did they show him?
As a mindless fanatic.
Does it move or talk? I want to speak with you alone.
Good.
Have you lost your mind?
I will never leave you. Never.
Loras rots in a cell because of you. THe High Sparrow rules this city because of you. Our two ancient houses face collapse because of you and your stupidity.
That is hardly your concern.
We marched for you.
What have they done to you?
You will leave for Highgarden today. There is no law that says you must stay here.
Do shut up, dear.
The last time a Tyrell came to Dorne, he was assassinated. 100 red scorpions, was it?
You murder your own prince, but you expect me to trust you?
And what is my heart’s desire?
Anything from you?
Cersei stole the future from me. She killed my son. She killed my grandson. She killed my granddaughter. Survival is not what I’m after now.
No? Good. Let the grown women speak.
Obara. You look like an angry little boy. Don’t presume to tell me what I need.
What is your name again? Barbaro?

He's a clever man, your Hand.  I've known a great many clever men.  I've outlived them all.  Do you know why?  I ignored them.  The lords of Westeros are sheep.  Are you a sheep?  No.  You're a dragon.  Be a dragon.
Peace.  Do you think that's what we had under your father or his father or his?  Peace never lasts, my dear.  Would you take a bit of advice from an old woman?
So, your master plan is to use our armies?  Forgive me for asking, but why did you bother to bring your own?

That's very nice to hear.  Of course, I can't remember a queen who was better loved than my grand daughter.  The common people loved her, the nobles loved her.  And what is left of her now?  Ashes.  Commoners, nobles, they're all just children really.  They won't obey you unless they fear you.
Then how do you mean to take the Iron Throne?  By asking nicely?
And you took your army, your real army, and went where they weren't?

He really was a cunt, wasn't he?  I did unspeakable things to protect my family, or watched them being done on my orders.  I never lost a night's sleep over them.  They were necessary.  And whatever I imagined necessary for the safety of House Tyrell I did.  But your sister has done things I wasn't capable of imagining.  That was my prize mistake, a failure of imagination.  She's a monster, you do know that?

I'd hate to die like your son clawing at my neck, foam and bile spilling from my mouth, eyes blood red, skin purple.  It must have been horrible for you as a Kingsguard, as a father.  It was horrible enough for me.  A shocking scene.  Not at all what I intended.  You see, I'd never seen the posion work before.  Tell Cersei.  I want her to know it was me.

If he was so clever why didn't he take Highgarden the moment your gold mines ran dry?  I suppose I'll be able to ask him myself soon enough.  No more learning from my mistakes, eh?  How will you do it?  With that sword?
That was Joffrey's sword, wasn't it?  Not that he ever used it.  What did he call it?
And now the rains weep for our whores.  Did we fight well?
How will it happen?
It was never our forte.  Golden roses indeed.  Your brother and his new queen thought you would be defending Casterly Rock.
It's done?
She's a disease.  I regret my role in spreading it.  You will too.
So you'll just let them take it?
That's good.

What better person to discuss it with?  What better guarantee could you have that the things you say will never leave this room?  But perhaps you're right.  If she's driven you this far it's gone beyond your control.
Will there be pain?
Yes.  You must be very wise by now.
You love her.  You really do love her.  You poor fool.  She'll be the end of you.
Mother says it's time to eat.
Potatoes.
Yes. Yes.
I can fight.
I was!
It’s too heavy.
I'm the sword in the darkness, the watcher on the walls, the shield that guards the realm of men. I pledge my life -
Night gathers and now my watch begins -
They burned my village. They put an arrow through my father's head right in front of me. They butchered my mother, everyone I ever knew.
Will there be anything else you need, Lord Commander?
You don't mean it, do you? Telling the wildlings you want to make peace. You're just doing it to trick them.
The one he’s travelling with, the one with the red beard, he led the raid on my village.
But what if we let the wildings through the gates and they cut our throats while we sleep?
Figured you were hungry.
He’s going up to Hardhome to save all them wildlings. Wildlings killed my mom and dad. They killed everyone in my village.
How can he trust him?
I heard what happened.
It’s about the Lord Commander.
Olly.
This wasn’t a fight. We were farmers and they slaughtered us. So why’s he saving them?
Wanted to ask you something. (Olly looks at Gilly) If you’ve got the time.
You believe that?
For the Watch.
Lord Commander. It’s one of the wildlings you brought back. Says he knows your Uncle Benjen. Says he’s still alive.
Says he was first Ranger. Said he knows where to find him.
Guymon! (to OLLY) Hide. Hide!
I should like to see you spar with a proper partner, ser.
Olyver, if it please you.
They rarely do, in my experience.
Well fought, ser.
Well, it took long enough. It seems our Knight of Flowers is engaged to be wed.
Know what?
With the husbands.
I'm afraid I'm not an offer, my lord.
Sirs, if you follow me, I'll arrange for a private room.
Very good, my lady.
(Gasps)
A term of courtesy in this establishment.
Any of the others?
Can't say I have. I'm wildly expensive.
My lord.
Which way do you like it?
I'm sorry. You're lovely, I just never acquired the taste.
Did you?
Everyone has a preference.
I hope I have as much stamina when I'm your age.
You like them both the same? Boys and girls?
Olyvar, my lady.
Yes. Yes, Sandstone. Just there.
It does. That’s the Sunspear bit right there. And that’s where the mountains are. And over here is… Sunsnake? Stonespear?
It looks like Dorne.
We should go there. I think we would have a lovely time.
(Leaning forward). Two is extra, you realize?
You have served us well, my son.
What are you doing? This establishment belong to Lord Petyr Baelish. You can't -
Where is the Maiden?
Which of the Seven will you worship today?
(Angrily). This is Lord Petyr Baelish's establishment.
He has a birthmark, your grace. Quite high on his thigh, wine-colored, and roughly the shape of Dorne.
Yes, very well. He is Ser Loras Tyrell, heir to Highgarden.
Yes, yes, Queen Margaery. She walked in on us once not long ago. She didn’t seem surprised.
I squired for him. He took a liking to me. He summoned me to his chamber the first day we met.
That night and many others.
We engaged in intimate relations.
Dead crows.
The Fist of the First Men.
How will we see your signal?
There was.
Aye, Castle Black. Everyone knows Castle Black. Which others?
How often do these patrols go out?
Just 'cause you want him inside you don't make him one of us.
Liar.
Which three?
You sure of that?
You'd like to know that.
No!
We've gotta cut them loose!
Your flock gets smaller every year.

People work together when it suits them. They're loyal when it suits them. They love each other when it suits them. And they kill each other when it suits them. She knows that, you don't. Which is why you'll never hold onto her.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Cut her loose, too. Do you see her sulking about it? That's because she understands the way things are.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I've seen you two whispering in the night, giggling like a pair of girls.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: She's right, you don't.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You think he loves you, is that it? What did he tell you? He's gonna make you his lady and live with you in some castle?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Cause he's pretty?  Is that it? You like his pretty hair and his pretty eyes? You think pretty is gonna make you happy?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Of course I'm jealous. You should be with one of your own.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You won't love him so much when you find out what he really is.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You would if you were mine. I'd tell you that you're beautiful and fierce and wild. I'd be good to you. You love him?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Not today they don’t.  He’s selling horses, he’s got some gold in there.
[Looks at JON] Make the crow kill him. [Walks towards JON]  You’re one of us now.  Prove it.
[To TORMUND] I heard shouting up there.
Cut his throat or he’ll tell the crows we’re here.
He’s a crow.  He’ll always be a crow.  And here’s his crow wife guarding him.  He’ll stab us in the back first chance he gets.
I keep telling you.
I know the difference between shouting and thunder.
Let’s carve him up.
Only one old man and eight good horses.
Told ya.  He’s still one of them.
Give me my life, my lord, and I’m yours.
Oh, that’s a pretty pin. Silver.
All alone in the deep dark woods.
the boy’s worth nothing dead. Benjen Stark’s own blood. Think what Mance would give us.
Couldn't say, My Lord. Don't know what "impunent" means.
They wasn't gone, old man. They was sleeping.
Trust me, you've never heard where I'm from neither.
What's that got to do with you? If your father's lord, how can you be lord too?
And what's that?
Didn't mean to come here. Meant to get much further South than this.
I just don't understand how you Southerners do things.
I'm not talking about Owls and Shadowcats.
I'm used to men who could chew that boy up and pick their teeth with his bones.
I'm used to worse than him.
So you're not lord now?
There's things that sleep in the day and hunt at night.
They far away?
Where is it you come from?
Why?
You're from South of the wall. That makes you a Southerner to me.
Well, there's a big man.
You asked them, they're answering you.
You hear them, boy? The Old Gods are answering you.
Giants and worse than giants.
They're my Gods too.
You both miss him. lt's only natural he should be in your thoughts and dreams, but that doesn't mean that –
Your father's not down there, little lord. Not for many years yet.
l'm not afraid of some hole in the ground.
That beast is supposed to be chained in the kennels!
You see? He's not here.

Did they? (Hodor is kneeling next to the pool in the godswood. Osha begins unstrapping Bran from Hodor’s back). I heard some other fools say it's Lannister Red. Means the Lannisters will rule all Seven Kingdoms before long. I heard a stable boy say it's the color of blood, to mark the death of your father.
The stars don't fall for men. The red comet means one thing, boy. Dragons.
Boil this for an hour and drink the tea. Makes all your pain go away.
Everyone dreams.
Lucky for you.
You've been having those dreams again.
Must not mean anything, then.
So, you've been dreaming of a three-eyed raven again? In the godswood, you told me you didn't dream. Now who's a liar? What did you see in your dream?
Something bad? Tell me, boy.
You might be a little lord, but don't you call me a liar.
Ask your Maester Luwin. He's the one studying books all the time.
I never said I didn't.
I've got to get these potatoes to the kitchen. Otherwise they'll put me in chains again.
That's not the same as being a liar.
The sea is hundreds of miles away.
They say all sorts of crazy things north of the Wall.
I done what I had to do to stay alive. I hate the Starks as well as you do. Let me serve you, my lord.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: It's your dream, little lord. The ocean has come to swallow this place. I ain't letting it drown me.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: There are other ways to serve, my lord.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: There are other ways to serve, my prince.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: We know things, the free people.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I already had that.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I have something for you from Prince Theon.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I'm no liar.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: It comes at a price.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: It was Robb Stark who put me in the kitchens. Put a spear in my hand again.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Let me serve you.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Me. He says I'm to make the rounds. And keep you warm.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: My Lord Greyjoy.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Other things.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Savage things.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Step aside.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: What all free people want. My freedom.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: You were here the day I was taken.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Even you, sweet giant.
We got a good start on them, but we can't outrun hounds forever.
You've never seen me climb a tree, little man.
The boy can't survive on walnuts.
Those people all love you. They would have given you whatever you needed.
We should have took more food.
We've been walking since before sunrise. Even Hodor will tire. Hodor:  Hodor.
Got as far as the farm and then doubled back. Walked through the stream to throw off the hounds. Hoped this would be the last place they'd think to look.
He killed them and burned them and passed them off as the little lords?
They'll never hear it from me. The little lads have suffered enough.
Who was that out there hung up at the gates?
I've got no great love for my own kind. I'll get you milk of the poppy. Tell me where to find it. LUWIN I don't want milk of the poppy.
North's the wrong way. And mother and brother are south. We don't know where.
Isn't he ashamed, your brother, needing you to protect him?
Not another step, boy. Unless you want to drown in your own blood.
I know you didn't, little lord.
If you kill me, that wolf will tear you to bits.
Rickon, stay with us.
Some good he is, then.
Were you inside the wolf again, little lord?
And who told you that? The three-eyed raven tell you?
Any boy his age who needs his sister to protect him is gonna find himself needing lots of protecting.
He's back.
I don't know what other people know and what they don't. I only know the Wall is a long way off.
I don't want to hear about it.
Looking for food.
That was poor planning.
We need to move.
We've got plenty of worries. We don't need to pour black magic on top of them.
What's he telling him?
I said so, didn't I?
If I had a bow, I could have shot a dozen rabbits.
Lady Reed here's got a stick so far up her ass, it's a wonder her feet touch the ground.
Me? It's my fault? First time I met her, she put a knife to my throat.
You grow up north of the Wall, you learn the proper way to handle game.
You learn how to use your fists, though.
You've got a big mouth, girl, and too many teeth.
Don't push me.
I had someone else in mind.
Not by the looks of it.
Some of us are just a little better.
That's not how you skin a rabbit.
You're a good little hunter.

Is that what he's telling you? That it's all for a reason? All these bad things happened because the gods got big plans for you? I wish it were true, little lord. But the gods wouldn't spare a raven's cold shit for you or me or anyone.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Go on, then. Tell us what it means.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I knew he'd come back. And he did. He came in through the back of the hut. Only it wasn't Bruni. Not really. His skin was pale like a dead man's. His eyes bluer than clear sky. He came at me, grabbed me by the neck and squeezed so hard I could feel the life slipping out of me. I don't know how I got the knife. When I did, I stuck it deep into his heart. And he hardly seemed to notice. I had to burn our hut down with him inside. I didn't ask the gods what it meant. I didn't need to. I already knew. It meant the North was no place for men to be. Not anymore. I promised your maester I'd get you to Castle Black and no further.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: Oh, no. I'm not going back there. Your brother is at Castle Black. That's where we're going. Look at me.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You don't know. None of you know. None of you have been up there. I had a man once. A good man. Bruni, his name was. I was his and he was mine. But one night Bruni disappears. People said he left me, but I knew him. He'd never leave me. Not for long.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: He can speak for himself.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: I don't want you talking to him anymore. Until we get to your brother at Castle Black.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: It's not all right. You think I can't hear you every day?  Filling his head with black magic. Talking about visions and three-eyed ravens and worse.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: So what do you talk about?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What are you telling him?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What did you say?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: What's he going on about? And how come he always gets to sit and chat while we do all the work?
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: You have a family. You need to go back to Castle Black so you can get back to them where you belong.
EpisodeName: The Bear and the Maiden Fair

Text: It’s just thunder, sweet giant.
Listen to me, little lord.
Say your good-byes, little man.
Shhh.  [She grabs RICKON by the waist and sits him on her lap]  Come here, little soldier.  You and me, we’re gonna have some adventures.

Your family took me in and was good to me when they had no cause to be.  [To RICKON] Shhh.  We’ll be fine, you and me.  The Umbers are great warriors.  Even I heard about them growing up.  They’ll teach you how to swing a sword.
[Looks at the Reeds] Keep this one safe.  [MEERA gives a slight nod]  He means the world to me.
[To BRAN and RICKON]  Where’s Shaggydog and Summer?
Got in a boat and rowed past the Bay of Seals.
He’ll be heading back to Castle Black then.  That’s where we should go.
I already told you.  I’m not going north of the Wall.
I learned to walk in darkness.
Some climb straight up the ice.
That’s a horse dying.
They’re long gone.
We can drink some blood while we wait.  I don’t need much.
Where are we?
You need to keep quiet.
You Southerners build your big castles and you never move.  You’re easy to find.
He'd fetch a good price to the right buyer. I served his family a long time. Didn’t get no wages. Way I see it, I’m owed.
Aye. They put me in chains and put a sword at my throat, so I’ve served them.
Same thing men always want. And when they really want it, they give it a bath first.
A lord?
I can give you what you want.
That so?
The flayed man.
Then I’ve seen worse.
You eat them after?
Builders!
You knelt as boys.
Snow's not wrong, My Lord.
The other one is Jafer Flowers, My Lord, less the hand the wolf tore off.
What smell?
You heard him.
Three dozen bodies with slit throats tossed off the top of the Wall. Seems like that would be a good lesson.
We need to teach them a lesson about the way we deal with their kind.
(Pauses.) No.
Aye.
My mother's still living at White Harbor. Could you write her? Tell her I died fighting the wildings.
“We fight and die for your glory, O glorious queen.”
You and you, you, you two, you, you, and you.
Move yourselves!
Prepare yourselves.
Valar morghulis.
Your Grace. Your Future Grace. You honor us all.
I fight and die for your glory, oh glorious queen.
Put him down!
Traitor! Coward!
Traitor! Traitor!
As you will. But still, we'd best make our plans.
I'll have to borrow it. The Lannisters will accommodate, I expect. We already owe Lord Tywin 3 million gold. What's another 80,000?
I'll try to keep you alive, for her sake. A fool's task, admittedly, but I've never been able to refuse your wife anything.
Mmm, how much?
Well well, this is an historic day. Something you don't know that I do. There's only one dagger like this in all of the Seven Kingdoms. It's mine.
Which Lord Tyrion will say was stolen from him. The only man who could say otherwise has no throat, thanks to your boy's wolf.
At least it was, until the tournament on Prince Joffrey's last nameday. I bet on Ser Jaime in the jousting, as any sane man would. When the Knight of the Flowers unseated him, I lost this dagger.
But we have a Kingdom to look after. I've hoped to meet you for some time, Lord Stark. No doubt Lady Catelyn has mentioned me.
It wasn't the man that I chose, my Lord. It was Catelyn Tully. A woman worth fighting for, I'm sure you'll agree.
The Starks... Quick tempers, slow minds.
A dear friend told me.
All too well. I still carry a token of his esteem from navel to collarbone.
Cat! Go on. Go upstairs. (to his whores)
Don't tell anyone. I have a reputation to maintain.
Good news? Perhaps you'd like to share it with your wife?
I meant no disrespect to you of all people.
I thought that she'd be safest in here. One of several such establishments I own.
I'm telling you the Crown is six million in debt.
Is she?
No one will come looking for you here. Isn't that what like you wanted? I'm truly sorry... about the locale.
The Master of Coin finds the money. The King and the Hand spend it.
The mere suggestion that the Queen's brother tried to kill your boy would be considered treason.
Tyrion Lannister. The Imp.
We are the lords of small matters here.
He never stops. Do you know Ser Hugh of the Vale? Not surprising. Until recently, he was only a squire... Jon Arryn's squire. He was knighted almost immediately after his master's untimely death.
I have my observers, as I said, and it's possible that they saw Lord Arryn visit this armorer several times in the weeks before his death.
No, it's quite all right. When I was a child I was very small and I come from a little spit of land called The Fingers, so you see, it's an exceedingly clever nickname.
No, please don't. If the Hound so much as heard you mention it, I'm afraid all the knights in King's Landing would not be able to save you.

Not what you were expecting? Has anyone ever told you the story of the Mountain and the Hound? Lovely little tale of brotherly love. The Hound was just a pup, six years old maybe. Gregor a few years older, already a big lad, already getting a bit of a reputation. Some lucky boys just born with a talent for violence. One evening... Gregor found his little brother playing with a toy by the fire... Gregor's toy, a wooden knight. Gregor never said a word, he just grabbed his brother by the scruff of his neck and shoved his face into the burning coals. Held him there while the boy screamed, while his face melted. There aren't very many people who know that story.

The wiser answer was no, my Lord. Get a message to this paragon of yours... Discreetly. Send him to question Ser Hugh. After that, you might want him to visit a certain armorer in the city. He lives in a large house at the top of the street of Steel.

A singularly bad idea. Do you see that boy there? One of Varys's little birds. The Spider has taken a great interest in your comings and goings. Now look there. That one belongs to the Queen. And do you see that Septa pretending to read her book?
An old friend of the family. I've known your mother a long long time.
And every inn in the city is full and the whores are walking bow-legged.
Distrusting me was the wisest thing you've done since you climbed off your horse.
I hear you're reading a boring book.
I promised Cat that I'd help you.
I will?
Lover's quarrel?
No. She's one of mine. Is there someone in your service whom you trust completely?
Ser Gregor Clegane. They call him the Mountain. The Hound's older brother.
Ser Hugh of the Vale. He was Jon Arryn's squire. Look how far he's come.
Am I? All those birds that whisper in your ear, such pretty little things. Trust me, we accommodate all inclinations.

And not long after that when I saw you escorting a certain foreign dignitary... Council business? Of course you would have friends from across The Narrow Sea. You're from there yourself, after all. We're friends, aren't we, Lord Varys? I'd like to think we are. So you can imagine my burden, wondering if the King might question my friend's sympathies… To stand at a crossroads where turning left means loyalty to a friend, turning right, loyalty to the realm.
And tell me, Lord Renly, when will you be having your friend?
Brothels make a much better investment than ship, I've found. Whores rarely sink.
Does it matter? If you fuck enough women, some of them will give you presents.
His Grace went on about you at some length after you took your leave. The word "treason" was mentioned.
I'm a purveyor of beauty and discretion, both equally important.
If you're still here come nightfall, I'll take you to see the last person Jon Arryn spoke with before falling ill. If that sort of thing still interests you.
Jorah Mormont's a slaver, not a traitor. Small difference, I know, to an honorable man.
Loras knew his mare was in heat. Quite crafty, really.
Strictly speaking, such a thing would not be in accordance with the King's laws.
Tell me. Does someone, somewhere, keep your balls in a little box? I've often wondered.
Well, he has more than you, for a start.
What's the meaning of this, Lannister?
When you find yourself in bed with an ugly woman, best close your eyes, get it over with. Cut her throat. Be done with it.
Yes. Earlier today, I distinctly recall seeing you talking to Lord Stark in his chambers.
100 gold dragons on the Mountain.
All desires are valid to a man with a full purse.
But you know something? I do believe that I have seen you even more recently than you have seen me.
Council business. We all have so much to discuss with Ned Stark.
He was the King's Hand. Perhaps Robert wanted them looked after. He was overcome with fatherly love.
It won't take more than an hour. But as you please.
My Lords! I'll bring the City Watch!
No honor but quite a bit of gold.
Now what will I buy with 100 gold dragons? A dozen barrels of Dornish wine? Or a girl from the pleasure houses of Lys?
One shudders at the thought.
Saw me with your own eyes?
Since you last saw me or since I last saw you?
To a small council meeting?
To find myself in a position where a simple word to the King --
When do you return to Winterfell?
You do move quietly.
You look a bit lonely today. You should pay a visit to my brothel this evening. First boy is on the house.
A bold move, my Lord, and admirable. But is it wise to yank the lion’s tail? Tywin Lannister is the richest man in all the Seven Kingdoms. Gold wins wars, not soldiers.
(whisper) That sounds like someone we know. The Mountain.
I’ve heard him called “Tywin Lannister’s mad dog.” I’m sure you have as well. (whispering to Ned) Can you think of any reason the Lannisters might possibly have for being angry with your wife?
It would be almost as brazen as attacking the Hand of the King in the streets of the capital.
Let's start over, shall we? You be the man and you be the woman.
No, not really. Impeccable bloodlines though.
Oh, everything, my dear. Everything there is.
So why did you call me here? Not for my wisdom, clearly.
We only make peace with our enemies, my Lord.
You'll need someone to share these burdens. I assure you, my price would be modest.
A stupid saying.
All is accomplished. The City Watch is yours.
And he cannot take the throne without your help.
For many years. Most of my life really.
I did warn you not to trust me.
I'm saving myself for another.
Look at you. You know what you want me to do.
My Lord protector.
Now!
Oh no. He got himself killed before the wedding.
Only if we lose.
So it will be Stannis and war?
So it would seem. Unless...
So when the King dies...
You are now Hand of the King and Protector of the Realm.
A naked Knight, apparently.
The girl is innocent, Your Grace.
Do you deny your father's crime?
A useful talent, l'm sure you'd agree.
And what would you do, my friend, if you found yourself sitting up there?
Must be strange for you, even after all these years… a man from another land, despised by most, feared by all.
When they castrated you, did they take the pillar with the stones? l've always wondered.
But you carry on whispering in one king's ear and then the next. l admire you.
l picture a gash. Like a woman's. ls that about right?
Long may he reign. My king. (bows)
lt's hard for them to simper and bow without heads.
Playing our roles.
You must be one of the few men in the city who isn't a man.
If she's escaped the capital, Winterfell seems the logical destination.
Of course, Your Grace.
We have enough wheat for a five-year winter. If it lasts any longer, (Shrugs indifferently), we'll have fewer peasants.
Well, you could ask Varys where she is. He'll have an answer for you. Whether you believe it,,, Myself, I have always had a hard time trusting eunuchs. (Smiles) Who knows what they want?

When boys and girls live in the same home, awkward situations can arise. Sometimes, I've heard, even brothers and sisters develop certain affections. And when these affections become common knowledge, well, that is an awkward situation, indeed. Especially in a prominent family. But prominent families often forget a simple truth, I've found.
Curious.
I'm glad you like it. Some people are fortunate enough to be born into the right family. Others have to find their own way.
Knowledge is power.
Yes.
Your Grace.
Ah, yes, that was poorly handled. Sometimes those with the most power have the least grace.
One moment, my friend. A moment.

You know, you remind me of another girl. A lovely thing I once acquired from an Elysium pleasure house. Beautiful, like yourself, and intelligent as yourself. But she wasn't happy. She cried, often. I asked her why but, we didn't have the kind of rapport that you and I have. Yes, it was quite sad. Girls from Elysium pleasure houses are expensive. Extremely expensive. And this one wasn't making me any money. I hate bad investments. Really, I do. They haunt me. I had no idea how to make her happy, no idea how to mitigate my losses. A very wealthy patron, he offered me a tremendous amount of money to let him transform this lovely, sad girl. To use her in ways that never occur to most men. And you know what occurs to most men. I would not say he succeeded in making her happy but my losses were definitely mitigated. (Stands up). Take tonight off to mourn Mhaegen’s child. I’ll see you tomorrow. But you’ll be happy? (Roz nods and smiles). That makes me happy.
Please accept my sincerest apologies. Ramka will take good care of you.
Shhh. Did he hurt you?
That's why they're called wildlings.
Who?
And Harrenhal, I suppose that's off the table as well.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: I don't appreciate being made a fool of, dwarf. If Myrcella marries the Martell boy, she can't very well marry Robin Arryn, can she?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: So did Janos Slynt, and he was given Harrenhal, too. Until you snatched it away.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: With a single stroke, you'd make me one of the greatest lords in the realm.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Yes, I could sing this song to Lysa, if I cared to. What's in it for me?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: (Nods.)
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: And I suppose you want me to broker this agreement?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Harrenhal is cursed.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Leave me out of your next deception.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Robb Stark will never release the Kingslayer.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: She imprisoned you. She tried to execute you. And you offer her son a princess?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: Tell no one what?
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: The Queen mustn't know.
EpisodeName: What Is Dead May Never Die

Text: "Your tent"? Not "Our tent"? The king snores, perhaps? Or simply prefers solitude? Pressures of command, no doubt. Four kings vying for the throne.
And on the night of your wedding, who was by the king's side then?
Cat, I've I've loved you since I was a boy. It seems to me that fate has given us this chance
Do you want to see your girls again? Sansa, more beautiful than ever? And Arya, just as wild as ever?

I understand that you don't like me, and while that saddens me greatly, I did not come here today seeking your affection. When you march on King's Landing, you may find yourself facing a protracted siege or open gates.
I've been unlucky in my affections, sadly.
If war were arithmetic, the mathematicians would rule the world. I did notice your brother entering His Grace's tent just now.
No, my lady.
Robb has surprised them all with his skills in battle, but he's not a mother. Consider it, Cat. You may not get another chance. I've brought you a gift.
You still have many friends at court, Your Grace, many who believe Ned Stark erred by not supporting your claim.
Your marriage is quite interesting. Not only to me, but to the realm. The marriage of a wealthy girl always breeds interest, if nothing else.
All of these tents look the same to me. -Would you be so kind
And who would you have me be loyal to? Your brother's corpse?
Betrayed? I wanted him to serve as Protector of the Realm. I begged him to seize the moment.
Both girls are healthy and safe. For now. But you know the Queen and you know Joffrey. I fear for their longevity if they remain in the capital.
I would give priority to my head.
I'm a practical man.
I'm not bringing these terms to him. I'm bringing them to you.
The Lannisters will trade your daughters for the Kingslayer.
You may have heard false reports.
Your Grace.
Your Grace.
Your husband was an honorable man. He should rest beside his family in the crypts beneath Winterfell. You may not believe
He was, Your Grace.

I have always found that to be the purest of motivations, but you won't have a chance to put your sword through Stannis, not today. You'll be cut to pieces before he sets foot on solid ground. If it is justice that you want, be smart about it.
My lord, my lady.
Stannis will be here in an hour. When he arrives, Renly's bannermen will flock to him. Your former companions will fight for the privilege of selling you to their new king.
Tell me, Ser Loras, what do you desire most in this world?
You will note that I am standing here talking to you, not Stannis.
Do you want to be a queen?
Stannis.
If you will allow me to represent your family's interests, I believe that an advantageous agreement TYWIN: The Tyrell host has returned to Highgarden?
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: On your son Tyrion's directive, I met with Catelyn Stark.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: They did. And perhaps that treason should be punished one day, after Stannis and Robb Stark are defeated.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Tonight, with your leave.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Yes, she does.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: He had an interesting proposal for her concerning her daughters.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: I traveled here directly from Renly Baratheon's camp.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: It is my belief that a moment of chaos affords opportunities lost soon after.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: It's only wine.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Lord Tywin.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: So they say. There has been talk of other forces at work. Dark forces.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: Thank you.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: The Tyrells have not yet declared for any of the surviving kings. Loras wants revenge. He blames Stannis for Renly's death. And Margaery
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: They have.
EpisodeName: The Old Gods and the New

Text: He'll let you go home? Joffrey's not the sort of boy who gives away his toys. You have a tender heart, just like your mother did at your age. I can see so much of her in you. She was like a sister to me. For her sake, I'll help get you home.
You honor me beyond words, Your Grace. I shall have to acquire some sons and grandsons.  The crowd laughs
You shouldn't say that. You'll be good enough for many things. He'll still enjoy beating you. And now that you're a woman, he'll be able to enjoy you in other ways as well.
Look around you. We're all liars here. And every one of us is better than you.
My lady. My sincerest condolences.
I'm waiting for word on an assignment that will take me far away from the capital. When I set sail, I might be able to take you with me. But you'll need to be ready to leave on a moment's notice.
How do I know?
I saw your mother not long ago. She's very eager to see you. And your sister.
Lovely day for it. Watching the ships.
Might I speak with Lady Sansa alone for a moment?
You said King's Landing was your home. You are the property of the crown. Stealing you would be treason. If you were to tell just one person ...
Apparently, Her Grace believed that you two had some sort of special relationship.
For which I am extremely grateful to you, my lord.  Lady Arryn and I have known each other since we were children. She has always been positively predisposed toward me.
Thank you, my dear.
They're only numbers. Numbers on paper. Once you understand that, it's easy to make them behave. Trivial even. You want a real challenge? Try whores.
Well, you are richer than I am.
I hear you owe that boy a significant debt.
I know.
I know. But how would the queen get that idea?
I'm surprised you're surprised. This is the safest place in the city.
If only. I owe you a significant debt. Our redheaded friend. You secured her release when the queen detained her.
Keep a low profile.
Titles do seem to breed titles.
You should have him knighted.
I hope you know that I'm your friend, Sansa. Your true friend.
If you wish to stay, then of course you will stay.
It doesn't matter what we want, once we get it, then we want something else. Your hair is different.
It's not a question of what I want. It's what you want. You want to go home, don't you?
Myself, I often find them a hindrance.
Of course, Your Grace.
She may not be the grandest ship in the world or the fastest, but she's mine. I've always wanted a ship. Now I want a dozen. Strange, isn't it?
I assure you that it will.
I have good news. I'll be leaving the city soon.
I see. And have you shared these suspicions with Lord Tywin?
I shall do my best.
Is he? And who's the lucky girl?
Lady Margaery wears it that way.
Petyr.
That didn't take long.
We'll speak again when I return.
Your Grace.
(voice over) And some are given a chance to climb, but they refuse. They cling to the realm or the gods or love. Illusions. Only the ladder is real. The climb is all there is.
Early days, my friend. It is flattering, really you feeling such dread at the prospect of me getting what I want.

You're so right. For instance, when I thwarted your plan to give Sansa Stark to the Tyrells. If I'm going to be honest, I did feel an unmistakable sense of enjoyment there. But your confidant, the one who fed you information about my plans, the one you swore to protect, you didn't bring her any enjoyment. And she didn't bring me any enjoyment. She was a bad investment on my part. Luckily, I have a friend who wanted to try something new. Something daring. And he was so grateful to me for providing this fresh experience.
(voice over) Many who try to climb it fail and never get to try again. The fall breaks them.
Chaos isn't a pit. Chaos is a ladder.
The realm? Do you know what the realm is? It's the thousand blades of Aegon's enemies. A story we agree to tell each other over and over till we forget that it's a lie.
There aren't There aren't even 200. I've counted.
Come, my lady. I know you've had a difficult day. But you're safe now. I promise you that. You're safe with me and sailing home.
I know. I know. But you must admit it looks suspicious. The king who executed your father, who tormented you for years, and you fled the scene of his murder.
Petyr... Are you hurt, my lady?
Saved you? My lady, he followed my orders. Every one of them. And he did it all for gold. Money buys a man's silence for a time. A bolt in the heart buys it forever.
Shh. You don't want the queen to hear, do you? 1,000 gold cloaks are searching for you. And if they found you, how do you think they would punish the girl who murdered the king?
Softly, my friend... Voices carry over water.
Yes, and he gave you a priceless necklace that once belonged to his grandmother. The last legacy of House Hollard.
Because he was a drunk and a fool and I don't trust drunk fools.
Good. Good. I'm sure you've had quite a fright. Rest easy. The worst is past.  Ser Dontos shouts up from his boat.
I had it made a few weeks ago. What did I once tell you about the capital?
A man with no motive is a man no one suspects. Always keep your foes confused. If they don't know who you are or what you want, they can't know what you plan to do next.
Everything. My friendship with the Lannisters was productive. But Joffrey, a vicious boy with a crown on his head, is not a reliable ally. And who could trust a friend like that?

I don't want friends like me. My new friends are predictable. Very reasonable people. As for what happened to Joffrey, well, that was something my new friends wanted very badly. Nothing like a thoughtful gift to make a new friendship grow strong.
And who helped me with this conspiracy?
Did I kill Joffrey? I've been in the Vale for weeks.
How do you know?
I'm getting married to your Aunt Lysa. She's waiting for us at the Eyrie. You'll be safe there.
So many men they risk so little. They spend their lives avoiding danger. And then they die. I'd risk everything to get what I want.
Then perhaps it was your husband.
Why not?
You're right. He wasn't involvedi n Joffrey's death. But you were. Do you remember that lovely necklace Dontos gave you? I don't suppose you noticed that a stone was missing after the feast.
Let me bathe and dress for the occasion. Once I'm presentable, I'll call on the septon immediately.
Tonight it is, then.
Arranging for the ascension of King Tommen I, extricating Sansa, getting her here alive.

The mountains are impassable. If you want to get to the Eyrie, you need to go through the Bloody Gate. It doesn't matter how large your army is, if you attack this gate, you do it on this road, three men abreast and get slaughtered like goats. The first Lords of the Vale didn't have much, but they had these mountains and they knew how to use them. And the fortress they built here has never been overcome. Not once in 1,000 years. Know your strengths, use them wisely, and one man can be worth 10,000.
Go. We'll speak soon.
I do think that we could wait until...
Like it was yesterday.
Lord Petyr Baelish and his niece Alayne.
My lady.
My lord.
Oh. I have brought you a gift.
Ought we not... inform the Lords of the Vale about the ceremony?
Pull up your hood. A memorable shade.
The deed is done. Faded into nothing. Only speaking of it can make it real.
You know what kind of stories poor men enjoy the most? Ones about rich girls they'll never meet.
Yes, I saw.
A lot can happen between now and never. If you want to build a better home, first you must demolish the old one.
I have only loved one woman... only one, my entire life. Your sister.

I loved your mother more than you could ever know. Given the opportunity, what do we do to those who've hurt the ones we love? In a better world, one where love could overcome strength and duty, you might have been my child. But we don't live in that world.
I'll send her away. I swear on my life. I swear to all the gods. Let her go, Lysa.
No, his mother should have a long time ago. Consider it a step in the right direction.
Oh, my sweet wife. My sweet, silly wife.
Call me Petyr.
Children.
Let her go.
Let me worry about Aunt Lysa.
Lysa! Let her go.
You're more beautiful than she ever was.
A girl with no learning and scattered wits. I assure you, she would be of no help.
A great-grandfather from Braavos, yes. I suppose all of our ancestors came from somewhere else originally.
First time I saw you, you were just a child. A girl from the North come to the capital for the first time. Not a child any longer. Why did you help me?

For Lysa, no. But for her son? Only 20 years ago, the knights of the Vale rode behind Jon Arryn, Ned Stark, Robert Baratheon. They fought together to overcome the Mad King. And since then, Arryn and Royce, Corbray, Waynwood, all the great houses of the Vale watched from the corner. A timid boy at a tavern brawl.
I adored Lysa with all my heart. But she loved her son so much, she became overprotective. He needs to learn how to swing a sword. How to ride a horse.
I want to know which side you're on. Do you support the Lannisters, the house that executed your friend Ned Stark?
I'd have you back Robin Arryn, Lord of the Vale.
No? Catelyn Stark begged her sister for support and Lysa refused. By staying out of the fray, you've allowed Tywin Lannister to destroy his rivals on every compass point. Who stands against him now?
The beauty of the Vale is matched only by the nobility of its great families. Lady Arryn often told me that you were her rock, Lord Royce.
Yes, it's time. Time for him to tour the Vale, to visit all the castles in his domain. Time for Robin to leave the nest.
You want justice, Lord Royce. I can hardly complain about that. I want the same.
And sickly little boys sometimes become powerful men.
Better to gamble on the man you know than the strangers you don't? And you think you know me?
Do you?
I don't know.

It wasn't safe for her inside. People die at their dinner tables. They die in their beds. They die squatting over their chamber pots. Everybody dies sooner or later. Don't worry about your death. Worry about your life. Take charge of your life for as long as it lasts. That is what it means to be Lord of the Vale.
Lady Arryn invited me. She and I have been close since childhood.
Let me fetch her.
My lady...
My lord...
Of course. Her suicide shattered us all.
She was always prone to melancholy. My lady wasn't meant for a world as brutal as ours.
That's not an answer.
You shouldn't be.
I have no doubt that upon my return, Robin’s skills will have improved immeasurably.
Lord Royce may be as honorable as he thinks he is, but he’s not alone in that castle. Do you trust all those knights and ladies, stable boys and serving girls?
No. But I pay them well, and they’ve seen what happens to men who disappoint me.
So a land so far from here, even Cersei Lannister can’t get her hands on you.
He has other gifts.
I did.
Lord Arryn will never be a great warrior.
Some boys develop more slowly. He’s still young.
The gift of a great name. Sometimes that’s all one needs.
We are.
Ah, yes. You were accused of killing him.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Because I am her uncle. I married her Aunt Lysa shortly before my beloved’s untimely death. We're family now, and you're an outsider. Forgive me, Lady Brienne, but experiences made me wary of outsiders.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Please, Lady Brienne. No need for such formality. (Brienne gets up) You are Catelyn Stark's sworn sword?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: We've met. With Renly Baratheon. What did he say about you? He said, "You're a loyalty comes free of charge." Someone appears to have paid quite a bit for it since then.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: A shadow? With a face? (scoffs) This woman swore to protect Renly; she failed. She swore to protect your mother; she failed. Why would I want somebody with your history of failure guiding Lady Sansa?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: An old saying. Inaccurate in this case.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: But you were accused.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Do you like the taste?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: It gives some men courage.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: My marriage proposal has been accepted.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Strange. I knew Cat from the time we were children. She never mentioned you.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Then what did happen?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: We don't want our new friend wandering the countryside alone. The roads of these parts aren't safe. Why don't you stay?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: You are becoming an observant young lady.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: (Grabbing her). I won't force you to do anything. Don't you know by now how much I care for you? Say the word and we turn the horses round, but listen to me. Listen. (Quietly). You've been running all your life. Terrible things happened to your family and you weep. You sit alone in a darkened room, mourning their fates. You've been a bystander to tragedy from the day they executed your father. Stop being a bystander, do you hear me? Stop running. There's no justice in the world. Not unless we make it. You loved your family. Avenge them.
(They begin walking). I assure you she's still a virgin. Tyrion never consummated the marriage. By the law of the land, she's no man's wife. Inspect her, if you must.
A message for me, you say. Strange that the seal is broken.
I've heard very little about you. Which makes you quite a rare thing, as lords go.
Queen Margaery adores Sansa. Cersei is Queen Mother, a title whose importance wanes with each passing day.

The Eyrie is mine. The last time the lords of the Eyrie formed an alliance with the lords of the North, they brought down the greatest dynasty this world has ever known. I'd like to borrow one of your birds. Cersei will expect a reply.

The Lannister name doesn't mean what it once did. Tywin is dead. He kept his house in power through sheer will. Without him, Jaime has one hand and no allies, Tommen is a soft boy, not a king to fear.
Yes. A bit shabby, isn't it? You've been here before.
You're not marrying Roose Bolton. No, you'll be marrying his son and heir, Ramsay. One day he'll be Warden of the North and you will -
(Shakes his head). No.
Always. You're a Stark. Dyeing your hair doesn't change that. You're Sansa Stark. The eldest surviving child of Ned and Catelyn Stark. Your place is in the North.
Every ambitious move is a gamble. You gambled when you drove a dagger into Robb Stark's heart. It appears that your gamble paid off. You're Warden of the North.
For now.
He did.
He seems pleased.
Home.
I hope so too. I've become quite fond of Lady Sansa during our travels together. She’s suffered enough.
Sansa -
Then I have delivered everything I've promised.
Winterfell is your home.
(They begin walking again). Stannis Baratheon garrisons at Castle Black. He'll march south to King's Landing before the winter snows block his way. But first, he has to take Winterfell.
He has a larger army. He's the finest military commander in Westeros. A betting man would put his money on Stannis. (They stop). As it happens, I am a betting man.

He should, he's a dangerous man. But even the most dangerous men can be outmaneuvered. And you’ve learnt to maneuver from the very best. (Puts a hand on her shoulder). I return before too long. (Puts a hand behind her head). You'll be strong without me. (She nods, and he kisses her). The North will be yours. Do you believe me?
I do. Once he liberates these lands from the Boltons he'll rally your father's bannermen to his cause. With the North behind him, Stannis can finally take the Iron Throne.
I know how hard it is to live with people you despise, believe me. But it won't be for long.

I saw her once. I was a boy, living with your mother's family. Lord Whent had a great tourney at Harrenhal. Everyone was there. The Mad King, your father, Robert Baratheon. And Lyanna, she was already promised to Robert. You can imagine what it was like for me, a boy from nowhere, with nothing to his name, watching these legendary men, tilting at the lists. The last two riders were Barristan Selmy and Rhaegar Targaryen. When Rhaegar won, everyone cheered for the prince. I remember the girls laughing when he took off his helmet and they saw that silver hair. How handsome he was. Until he rode right past his wife, Elia Martell, and all the smiles died. I've never seen so many people so quiet. He rode past his wife, and he lay a crown of winter roses in Lyanna's lap. Blue as frost. How many tens of thousands had to die because Rhaegar chose your aunt?
Stannis takes Winterfell, he rescues you from the most despised family in the North. Grateful for your late father's courageous support for his claim, he names you Wardeness of the North.
Then you will take this Bolton boy, Ramsay, and make him yours.
Cersei sent for me. (They stop). We must not let her sniff out any trouble.
Come. (Takes her arm). Let's speak somewhere the dead can't hear us. (They begin walking away).
I am.
I thought I might find you here. (Sansa turns to him). Your Aunt Lyanna.
King's Landing.
Of course you do. He's already fallen for you.
You are the last surviving Stark. He needs you.
Do you think it’s wise, your Grace, arresting the heir to Highgarden?
I would counsel patience, your grace.
My sources are well placed. They tell me Roose Bolton plans to marry her to his son, Ramsay, a bastard recently legitimized by King Tommen.
Perhaps I can help. The Knights of the Vale are some of the best fighters in Westeros, trained to battle in the ice and the snow.
Ser Jaime, then.

Stannis Baratheon is also a traitor, marching with his army on Winterfell. Let Stannis and Roose battle, let the enemies of the throne slaughter each other and when they’re done seize Winterfell from whichever thief survives.
That is why it is critical to strike soon, while the victor still licks his wounds. Surely your Uncle Kevan could muster a force.

There is another matter, your Grace. Something so urgent I couldn’t trust the words to a raven. You once charged me with finding Arya Stark. To my shame I failed you. But I have found Sansa Stark. Alive, and well, and home again. In Winterfell.
We both peddle fantasies, Brother Lancel. Mine just happen to be entertaining.
You wouldn’t risk a single Lannister soldier or a single coin from the Royal Treasury. What do you have to lose, a brothel keeper?
As I said. I live to serve.
House Tyrell won’t tolerate these insults.
I know how hard it can be to lose both of your parents at such a young age.
I’ll not rest until the lion flies over Winterfell.
I’m here on urgent business for the Queen Mother. Should I send word that I’ll be delayed?
Indeed. As reward for stabbing his own king in the heart.
Lysa was a good woman. A kind woman.
Marrying his son to the last of the Starks gives him more legitimacy in the North than an alliance with a hated southern house.
Name me Warden of the North.
Of course. And the Faith were armed on your orders.
One’s choice of companion is a curious thing.
That’s quite a family to abandon.
Well done.
Yes? (Lancel approaches him) Lancel Lannister.
Young Lord Robyn heeds my advice. And I have always counseled loyalty to the throne.

A piece of info that she know that I had. Neither silence nor lies were an option. But I have other information of which she is unaware. And as you say, our interests are aligned. I have a gift for you.
It was an establishment like no other. The sheer range of appetites catered to, desires that didn’t exist until we invented them.
Cersei summoned me to King’s Landing. I dared not refuse her.
I promise you..
I’m sorry about the locale.
It felt like the safest place.
The past is the past. The future is all that’s worth discussing. The future of House Tyrell.
The same kind I gave Cersei. A handsome young man.

I bring good news. My friends in the North tell me Sansa has escaped Winterfell. I expect she’s headed to Castle Black where her brother serves as Lord Commander. But she won’t be safe there, not with the Boltons after her.
Lord Royce has served the Vale well. He;s enjoyed a distinguished military career. If we could trust his absolute loyalty, he would make a capable commander in the wars to come.
On our way to the Fingers, we were set upon by a large force of Bolton men. Seemed to know exactly when we were traveling and exactly whom we were transporting.
Tell me, Lord Royce, how many people knew of my travel plans with Sansa Stark? I shared my intentions with you and no one else.
A gyrfalcon. The greatest and rarest of birds.
I missed your name day. Go on.
I think he deserves one more chance. What do you say?
Indeed I was.
My lord. Come and see.
Our lord has spoken. Gather the Knights of the Vale. The time has come to join the fray.
That was my instinct as well.
The Defender of the Vale!
Your home is the Vale. The Lord of the Vale stands before us. And only his judgment concerns me.
Do you want me to beg for my life? If that’s what you want, I will. Whatever you ask that is in my power, I will do.
I made a mistake, a horrible mistake. I underestimated a stranger.
Sansa, I --
Sansa. Lady Brienne. When I heard you had escaped WInterfell, I feared the worst. You have no idea how happy I am to see you unharmed.
And I will. You must believe me when I tell you that I will.
Did he cut you?
Half-brother.
He beat you.
I can’t begin to contemplate --
I didn’t know.
I rode north with the Knights of the Vale to come to your aid. They’re encamped at Moat Cailin as we speak.

I would do anything to undo what’s been done to you. I know that I can’t. Will you allow me to say one more thing before I go? Your great-uncle Brynden the Blackfish has gathered what remains of the Tully forces and retaken Riverrun. You might consider seeking him out. The time may come when you need an army loyal to you.
I’m so sorry.
Then I will die.
Your brother’s army.
Forgive me, my lady… if you’re at prayer.

No, you weren’t. Every time I’m faced with a decision, I close my eyes and see the same picture. Whenever I consider an action, I ask myself will this action help to make this picture a reality? Pull it out of my mind and into the world? And I only act if the answer is yes. A picture of me on the Iron Throne… and you by my side.

The past is gone for good. You can sit here mourning its departure or you can prepare for the future. You, my love, are the future of House Stark. Who should the North rally behind? A trueborn daughter of Ned and Catelyn Stark born here at Winterfell or a motherless bastard born in the south?
I thought you knew what I wanted.
News of this battle will spread quickly through the Seven Kingdoms. I’ve declared for House Stark for all to hear.
You were a child.
I want you to be happy. I want you to be safe.
I've heard she beat the Hound in single combat. She's a very impressive woman.
My lady.
My lady.
What about happy? Why aren't you happy? What do you want that you do not have?

I was sorry when he died.  Your father and I had our differences but he loved Cat very much.  So did I.  She wasn't fond of you, was she?  Well, it appears she vastly underestimated you.  Your father and brothers are gone, yet here you stand King in the North.  Last best hope against the coming storm.

Not even thank you?  If it weren't for me you'd have been slaughtered at that battlefield.  You have many enemies, My King, but I swear to you I'm not one of them.  I love Sansa as I loved her mother.
Forgive me.  We have never talked properly.  I wanted to remedy that.
I delivered his bones myself.  I presented them to Lady Catlyn as a gesture of goodwill from Tyrion Lannister.  It seems like a lifetime ago.  Do give Lord Tyrion my best when you see him.

Don't fight in the north or the south.  Fight every battle everywhere always in your mind.  Everyone is your enemy, everyone is your friend, every possible series of events is happening all at once.  Live that way and nothing will surprise you.  Everything that happens will be something that you've seen before.
One of two things will happen.  Either the dead will defeat the living, in which case all our troubles come to an end.  Or life will win out.  And what then?
Command suits you.  The northerns are all facing north worried about the threat beyond the wall.
I know Cersei better than anyone here.  If you turn your back on her --
I only meant to say --
I'm sorry to have disturbed you, Lord Stark.

No.  That very question was what started the War of the Five Kings.  In a way, that dagger made you what you are today.  Forced from your home, driven out to the wilds beyond the Wall.  I imagine you've seen things most men wouldn't believe.
The last man who wielded it meant to cut your throat, but your mother fought him off.

The other dagger, the one that took her life, I would have stopped that dagger with my own heart if I could have.  I wasn't there for her when she needed me most.  But I am here for her now.  To do what she would have done, to protect her children.  Anything I can do for you, Brandon, you need only ask.
To go through all of that and make your way home again only to find such chaos in the world, I can only imagine --
This is for you.
Are you sure this is the only copy at Winterfell?
Lady Stark thanks you for your service.
An alliance makes sense. Together, they'd be difficult to defeat. He was named King.
And after she murders you, what does she become?
If we could speak alone, I can explain everything.
Lady Sansa, forgive me I'm a bit confused.
Lady Sansa, I have known you since you were a girl. I've protected you.
Only by reputation. They worship the God of Death, I believe. I never trust godly men.
Sansa, I beg you! I loved your mother since the time I was a boy.
Sansa, please.
You are family, too. Would Arya really murder her own sister?
Give me a chance to defend myself. I deserve that.
I am Lord Protector of the Vale and I command you to escort me safely back to the Eyrie.
I can't believe he'd surrender the Northern crown without consulting you.
I deny it! None of you were there to see what happened. None of you knows the truth.
I did it to protect you.
I know of no such letter.
I loved you. More than anyone.
I've heard gossip that the Dragon Queen is quite beautiful.
It's not easy for ravens to fly in these storms. Perhaps Jon tried to send word earlier.
Jon is young and unmarried. Daenerys is young and unmarried.
Sansa! (GASPS) I--
She's your sister
Whatever your aunt
Why did she come to Winterfell?
Why did she unearth the letter Cersei made you write?
The roads have gone right to hell, haven't they? Cracked three spokes this morning.
Got to get this salt pork to The Twins in time for the wedding.
Many thanks.
Need about eight hands.
Apologies, my lord.
More wine?
Yes, my lord.
My lord.
No, my lord.
Yes, my lord?
Yes, my lord.
(nods)
Apologies, ser.
He said it was a matter of life and death.
Lord Tyrion sent me.
Ser Bronn of the Blackwater was anointed by the king himself.
Ser Bronn. Ser Bronn.
Yes, my lord?
Yes, my lord.
Yes, my lord.
My lord, I don't even
No, my lord.
They wouldn't take it, my lord.
Uh, my lord
Yes, my lord.
I didn't tell them anything.
Lots of things.
Pardon, my lady.
I don't think I can, my lord.
My lord, my lady. Your father has called a meeting of the small council.
I don't see it, my lord.
Wild lemons on a purple field, House Dalt of Lemonwood. A vulture grasping a baby in it's talons, House Blackmont. A crowned skull, the Manwoodys of Kingsgrave.
Yes, my lord.
I'll clean it up.
Is it solid gold?
Any other witnesses, my lord?
Farewell, my lord.
I already gave them an answer, my lord.
I brought you some wine, my lord, but they took it from me.
I didn't tell them anything, my lord.
I'll ask, my lord.
I'm supposed to get a list of names from you, my lord. Anyone who might testify on your behalf.
I've already asked, my lord. They won't let him see you.
I've heard nothing, my lord.
My lord, she's gone.
No, my lord. You didn't?

There's something else, my lord. A man, I didn't know his face, he came to ask if I'd testify against you. Said I'd be named Ser Podrick Payne if I told the judges you'd bought a poison called the Strangler.
They didn't find the candles, though. A quill, some parchment, duck sausage, almonds, and some hard cheese.
Yes, my lord?
You've been good to me, my lord.
And Prince Oberyn of Dorne.
He's already been called as a witness for the queen.
Mace Tyrell.
My lord.
My lord.
No one's seen her since the wedding. You don't think she...
They say he's a known cutthroat and your close associate. He's under investigation himself.
Who will?
You're to stand trial in a fortnight for murdering the king.
Your father.
Come on, move.
I won't slow you down, ser... my lady. I promise I'll serve you well.
All knights have squires, my lady.
I swore an oath, my lady.
Never, my lady. I'm your squire.
No, my lady.
No, my lady.
No, my lady. That was the cooks.
No, no, no, no.
Oh, no, no, no.
Yes, my lady, when I was young. There wasn't much call for it with Lord Tyrion, though. He preferred litters.
A Kingsguard. He tried to kill Lord Tyrion at the Blackwater.
Hells. Gods.
I brought him his meals and cleared his table when he was finished. I kept his clothing and linens clean. Carried his messages and returned the replies. Mostly I poured wine.
I killed a man.
I know.
I pushed a spear through the back of his head.
I'm helping you with your armor.
That's a long way off.
They'll say I wasn't a very good squire.
Aye, my lady. Could be.
Couldn't agree more, my lady.
Don't want to offend, my lady. Truly, I don't.
Her aunt, Lysa Arryn. Catelyn Stark's sister. She's Arya's last living relative with money. She hates the Lannisters. Lord Tyrion made sure I knew which house married which and who hated who.
It's nothing, my lady.
No, my lady.
They'll take her to the Eyrie, my lady.
Yes, my lady.
No.
The Hound.
The Lannisters want Lady Sansa. The Lannisters have money. People kill for money. I don't think we should be telling people we're searching for Lady Sansa.
A figure-eight, like you taught me.
Ah, I think that way.
Thieves, maybe?
I hobbled them last night.
I was watching you I thought you migth need some help…
She was just here...
Well, where would I go?
Will we head north at some point? He said Sansa had a brother at Castle Black. We’re a few days ride from the Kingsroad, but that will take us--
An attendant to a knight?
But I’m you’re squire.
Sansa still might.
You swore to find the Stark girls.
(pause) No, my lady.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: 10, I think. Too many. My lady, I don't think this is the right--
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: My lady, if those Stark girls refused your service, maybe you’re released from your vow.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: She's dyed her hair, but it's her. She's sitting with Petyr Baelish.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Stop, stop, stop! (Horse stops) That’s a good boy.
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Yes, my lady?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

Text: Yes, my lady. (stands) Sansa Stark?
EpisodeName: The House of Black and White

EpisodeName: The House of Black and White
